Nonmetallic Structures.question: Laminated wood spars may be substituted for solid rectangular wood spars

choices.A: only in certain instances where the primary load is shared by one or more other original structural member.
choices.B: if the same quality wood is used in both.
choices.C: only upon specific approval by the manufacturer or the FAA.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Any wooden member that has been overstressed is subject to which type of failure?

choices.A: Stretch.
choices.B: Compression.
choices.C: Torsion.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which statement about wood decay is correct?

choices.A: Decay that occurs before the wood is seasoned does not affect the strength of the finished piece.
choices.B: A limited amount of certain kinds of decay is acceptable in aircraft woods since decay affects the binding between the fibers and not the fibers themselves.
choices.C: Decay is not acceptable in any form or amount.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which statement concerning wood decay is correct?

choices.A: Decay that appears to be mineral streaks is acceptable.
choices.B: A limited amount of decay along the beveled edges of unrouted beams is acceptable.
choices.C: Any form or amount of decay is not permitted.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of the following conditions will determine acceptance of wood with mineral streaks?

choices.A: Careful inspection fails to reveal any decay.
choices.B: They produce only a small effect on grain direction.
choices.C: Local irregularities do not exceed limitations specified for spiral and diagonal grain.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Pin knot clusters are permitted in wood aircraft structure provided

choices.A: they produce a small effect on grain direction.
choices.B: they have no mineral streaks.
choices.C: no pitch pockets are within 12 inches.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Laminated wood is sometimes used in the construction of highly stressed aircraft components. This wood can be identified by its

choices.A: parallel grain construction.
choices.B: similarity to standard plywood construction.
choices.C: perpendicular grain construction.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When patching a plywood skin, abrupt changes in cross-sectional areas which will develop dangerous stress concentration should be avoided by using

choices.A: circular or elliptical patches.
choices.B: square patches.
choices.C: doublers with any desired shaped patches.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Glue deterioration in wood aircraft structure is indicated

choices.A: when a joint has separated and the glue surface shows only the imprint of the wood with no wood fibers clinging to the glue.
choices.B: when a joint has separated and the glue surface shows pieces of wood and/or wood fibers clinging to the glue.
choices.C: by any joint separation.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Compression failures in wood aircraft structures are characterized by buckling of the fibers that appear as streaks on the surface

choices.A: at right angles to the growth rings.
choices.B: parallel to the grain.
choices.C: at right angles to the grain.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When and how is finishing tape applied on a fabric-covered aircraft?

choices.A: Sewed or laced on before dope is applied.
choices.B: Doped on immediately prior to the finish coat.
choices.C: Doped on after the first or second coat of dope.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The determining factor(s) for the selection of the correct weight of textile fabric to be used in covering any type of aircraft is the

choices.A: maximum wing loading.
choices.B: speed of the aircraft.
choices.C: speed of the aircraft and the maximum wing loading.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: How many fabric thicknesses will be found in a French-fell seam?

choices.A: Five.
choices.B: Three.
choices.C: Four.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Finishing tape (surface tape) is used for what purpose?

choices.A: To help prevent 'ripple formation' in covering fabric.
choices.B: To provide additional wear resistance over the edges of fabric forming structures.
choices.C: To provide additional anti-tear resistance under reinforcement tape.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Moisture, mildew, chemicals, and acids have no effect on

choices.A: glass fabric.
choices.B: cotton fabric.
choices.C: polyester fabric.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Fungicide and mildewicide additives are important for fabric covered aircraft, such

choices.A: cotton and dacron.
choices.B: cotton and linen.
choices.C: glass and linen.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The strength classification of fabrics used in aircraft covering is based on

choices.A: bearing strength.
choices.B: shear strength.
choices.C: tensile strength.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Fabric rejuvenator is used to

choices.A: restore the condition of dope coatings.
choices.B: restore fabric strength and tautness to at least the minimum acceptable level.
choices.C: penetrate the fabric and restore fungicidal resistance.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Machine-sewn seams in aircraft covering fabrics may be of which of the following types?

choices.A: Folded-fell or plain lapped.
choices.B: Folded-fell, French-fell, or plain lapped.
choices.C: French-fell or plain lapped.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When testing the strength of Grade A cotton fabric covering an aircraft that requires only intermediate grade, the minimum acceptable strength the fabric must have

choices.A: 70 percent of its original strength.
choices.B: 70 percent of the original strength for intermediate fabric.
choices.C: 56 pounds per inch warp and fill.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When dope-proofing the parts of the aircraft structure that come in contact with doped fabric, which of the following provide an acceptable protective coating?

choices.A: Resin impregnated cloth tape and cellulose tape.
choices.B: Aluminum foil and resin impregnated cloth tape.
choices.C: Aluminum foil and cellulose tape.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Hole filling fasteners (for example, MS20470 rivets) should not be used in composite structures primarily because of the

choices.A: possibility of causing delamination.
choices.B: increased possibility of fretting corrosion in the fastener.
choices.C: difficulty in forming a proper shop head.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Metal fasteners used with carbon/graphite composite structures

choices.A: may be constructed of any of the metals commonly used in aircraft fasteners.
choices.B: must be constructed of material such as titanium or corrosion resistant steel.
choices.C: must be constructed of high strength aluminum-lithium alloy.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Sandwich panels made of honeycomb construction are used on aircraft because this type of construction

choices.A: has a high tensile strength and is non-flammable.
choices.B: has a low weight and is corrosion resistant.
choices.C: saves weight while not compromising strength.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of these methods may be used to inspect fiberglass/honeycomb structures for entrapped water?

choices.A: Backlighting and acoustic emission monitoring.
choices.B: X-ray and acoustic emission monitoring.
choices.C: X-ray and backlighting.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When balsa wood is used to replace a damaged honeycomb core, the plug should be cut so that

choices.A: the grain is parallel to the skin.
choices.B: it is about 1/8 inch undersize to allow sufficient bonding material to be applied.
choices.C: the grain is perpendicular to the skin.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When repairing puncture-type damage to a metal-faced laminated honeycomb panel, the edges of the doubler should be tapered to

choices.A: two times the thickness of the metal.
choices.B: 100 times the thickness of the metal.
choices.C: whatever is desired for a neat, clean appearance.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: One of the best ways to assure that a properly prepared batch of matrix resin has been achieved is to

choices.A: perform a chemical composition analysis.
choices.B: have mixed enough for a test sample.
choices.C: test the viscosity of the resin immediately after mixing.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: How does acoustic emission testing detect defects in composite materials?

choices.A: By picking up the 'noise' of any deterioration that may be present.
choices.B: By analyzing ultrasonic signals transmitted into the parts being inspected.
choices.C: By creating sonogram pictures of the areas being inspected.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When conducting a tap test on a composite panel, which of the following sounds would indicate delamination?

choices.A: Sharp ringing.
choices.B: Dull thud.
choices.C: Sharp thud.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: One method of inspecting a laminated fiberglass structure that has been subjected to damage is to

choices.A: strip the damaged area of all paint and shine a strong light through the structure.
choices.B: use dye-penetrant inspection procedures, exposing the entire damaged area to the penetrant solution.
choices.C: use an eddy current probe on both sides of the damaged area.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When inspecting a composite panel using the ring test/tapping method, a dull thud may indicate

choices.A: less than full strength curing of the matrix.
choices.B: separation of the laminates.
choices.C: an area of too much matrix between fiber layers.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of the following are benefits of using microballoons when making repairs to laminated honeycomb panels?

choices.A: Improved strength-to-weight ratio, less density, and lower stress concentrations.
choices.B: Greater concentrations of resin in edges and corners.
choices.C: Less density and improved strength-to-weight ratio.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The length of time that a catalyzed resin will remain in a workable state is called the

choices.A: pot life.
choices.B: work life.
choices.C: plyable life.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The classification for high tensile strength fiberglass used in aircraft structures

choices.A: E-glass.
choices.B: S-glass.
choices.C: G-glass.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Superficial scars, scratches, surface abrasion, or rain erosion on fiberglass laminates can generally be repaired by applying

choices.A: a piece of resin-impregnated glass fabric facing.
choices.B: one or more coats of suitable resin (room-temperature catalyzed) to the surface.
choices.C: a sheet of polyethylene over the abraded surface and one or more coats of resin cured with infrared heat lamps.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The classification for fiberglass reinforcement material that has high resistivity and is the most common is

choices.A: E-glass.
choices.B: S-glass.
choices.C: G-glass.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Composite fabric material is considered to be the strongest in what direction?

choices.A: Fill.
choices.B: Warp.
choices.C: Bias.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: What reference tool is used to determine how the fiber is to be oriented for a particular ply of fabric?

choices.A: Fill clock (or compass).
choices.B: Bias clock (or compass).
choices.C: Warp clock (or compass).

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The strength and stiffness of a properly constructed composite buildup depends primarily on

choices.A: a 60 percent matrix to 40 percent fiber ratio.
choices.B: the orientation of the plies to the load direction.
choices.C: the ability of the fibers to transfer stress to the matrix.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which fiber to resin (percent) ratio for advanced composite wet lay-ups is generally considered the best for strength?
What is the material layer used within the vacuum bag pressure system to absorb excess resin during curing called?

choices.A: Bleeder.
choices.B: Breather.
choices.C: Release.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Proper prepreg composite lay-up curing is generally accomplished by

choices.A: adding a catalyst or curing agent to the resin.
choices.B: applying external heat and pressure.
choices.C: room temperature exposure and applying pressure.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When repairing large, flat surfaces with polyester resins, warping of the surface is likely to occur. One method of reducing the amount of warpage is to

choices.A: add an extra amount of catalyst to the resin.
choices.B: use short strips of fiberglass in the bonded repair.
choices.C: use less catalyst than normal so the repair will be more flexible.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When necessary, what type of cutting fluid is usually acceptable for machining composite laminates?

choices.A: Water soluble oil.
choices.B: Water displacing oil.
choices.C: Water only.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Fiberglass laminate damage not exceeding the first layer or ply can be repaired by

choices.A: filling with a putty consisting of a compatible resin and clean, short glass fibers.
choices.B: sanding the damaged area until aerodynamic smoothness is obtained.
choices.C: trimming the rough edges and sealing with paint.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Fiberglass damage that extends completely through a laminated sandwich structure

choices.A: may be repaired.
choices.B: must be filled with resin to eliminate dangerous stress concentrations.
choices.C: may be filled with putty which is compatible with resin.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Repairing advanced composites using materials and techniques traditionally used for fiberglass repairs is likely to result in

choices.A: restored strength and flexibility.
choices.B: slightly stronger strength to weight ratio.
choices.C: an unairworthy repair.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of the following, when added to wet resins, provide strength for the repair of damaged fastener holes in composite panels?

choices.A: Flox and chopped fibers.
choices.B: Microballoons.
choices.C: Microballoons and flox.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of the following are generally characteristic of aramid fiber (Kevlar) composites?

choices.A: High tensile strength and flexibility.
choices.B: High tensile strength and stiffness.
choices.C: Corrosive effect in contact with aluminum and ability to conduct electricity.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of the following are generally characteristic of carbon/graphite fiber composites?

choices.A: Flexibility, high compressive strength, and corrosive effect in contact with aluminum.
choices.B: Stiffness, high compressive strength, and corrosive effect in contact with aluminum.
choices.C: Ability to conduct electricity.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of the following drill bit types work best when drilling an aramid fiber (Kevlar) composite laminate?

choices.A: Tool steel with standard grind.
choices.B: Diamond dust coated.
choices.C: Carbide W-point.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which is correct concerning the use of a file?

choices.A: Apply pressure on the forward stroke, only, except when filing very soft metals such as lead or aluminum.
choices.B: A smoother finish can be obtained by using a double-cut file than by using a single-cut file.
choices.C: The terms 'double-cut' and 'second-cut' have the same meaning in reference to files.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which procedure is correct when using a reamer to finish a drilled hole to the correct size?

choices.A: Turn the reamer in the cutting direction when enlarging the hole and in the opposite direction to remove from the hole.
choices.B: Turn the reamer only in the cutting direction.
choices.C: Apply considerable pressure on the reamer when starting the cut and reduce the pressure when finishing the cut.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When straightening members made of 2024-T4, the aviation mechanic should

choices.A: straighten cold and reinforce.
choices.B: straighten cold and anneal to remove stress.
choices.C: apply heat to the inside of the bend.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Clad aluminum alloys are used in aircraft because they

choices.A: are naturally corrosion resistant, so they do not require corrosion resistant materials for protection.
choices.B: have surface layers of pure aluminum or aluminum alloy bonded to the core material to inhibit corrosion.
choices.C: are highly corrosion resistant because an oxide film forms on their surfaces upon contact with air.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Parts fabricated from Alclad 2024-T3 aluminum sheet stock must have all

choices.A: bends made with a small radius to develop maximum strength.
choices.B: bends 90 to the grain.
choices.C: scratches, kinks, tool marks, nicks, etc., must be held to a minimum.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The left brake is dragging excessively on an airplane on which no recent brake service work has been performed. The most probable cause is

choices.A: foreign particles stuck in the master cylinder compensating port.
choices.B: excessively worn brake linings.
choices.C: low fluid supply in the brake system reservoir.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which characteristics apply to aircraft hydraulic systems?

choices.A: About 80 percent operating efficiency (20 percent loss due to fluid friction).
choices.B: Minimum maintenance requirements and simple to inspect.
choices.C: Minimum maintenance requirements, lightweight, and simple to inspect.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Materials which are Skydrol compatible or resistant include

choices.A: epoxy paints and most common aircraft metals.
choices.B: polyurethane and epoxy paints.
choices.C: common aircraft metals, polyurethane, and epoxy paints.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which is true regarding the ground check of a flap operating mechanism which has just been installed?

choices.A: If the time required to operate the mechanism increases with successive operations, it indicates the air is being worked out of the system.
choices.B: If the time required to operate the mechanism decreases with successive operations, it indicates the air is being worked out of the system.
choices.C: All hydraulic lines and components should be checked for leaks by applying soapy water to all connections.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Composite oxygen bottles that conform to DOT-E-8162 have a service life of

choices.A: 5 years or 5,000 filling cycles whichever occurs first.
choices.B: 10 years or 5,000 filling cycles whichever occurs first.
choices.C: 15 years or 10,000 filling cycles whichever occurs first.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Cases for electrically operated instruments are made of

choices.A: Aluminum or composite cases.
choices.B: Iron or steel cases.
choices.C: Bakelite or plastic cases.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: In general, the purpose of an aircraft transponder is to

choices.A: continually transmit heading, speed, and rate of climb/descent, etc., information to ATC.
choices.B: monitor aircraft speed, heading, altitude, and attitude whenever the autopilot system is engaged.
choices.C: receive an interrogation signal from a ground station and automatically send a reply back.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: What is the primary purpose of the crossfeed system?

choices.A: It allows any tank to supply fuel to any engine.
choices.B: It bypasses the engine shutoff valve if it fails.
choices.C: It divides the fuel and sends it to the injectors.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: What must each fuel quantity indicator be calibrated to read during level flight when the quantity of fuel remaining is equal to the unusable fuel supply?

choices.A: The total unusable fuel quantity.
choices.B: Both the total unusable fuel quantity and the unusable fuel quantity in each tank.
choices.C: Zero.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Integral fuel tanks on transport aircraft are

choices.A: easily removed for service or inspection.
choices.B: constructed of plastic or fiberglass.
choices.C: supported by the aircraft structure.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: What precautions must be observed if a gravity-feed fuel system is permitted to supply fuel to an engine from more than one tank at a time?

choices.A: The tank airspaces must be interconnected.
choices.B: The fuel outlet ports of each tank must have the same cross-sectional area.
choices.C: Each tank must have a valve in its outlet that automatically shuts off the line when the tank is empty.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The pin section of an AN/MS connector is normally installed on

choices.A: the power supply side of a circuit.
choices.B: the ground side of a circuit.
choices.C: either side of a circuit (makes no difference).

Nonmetallic Structures.question: If several long lengths of electrical cable are to be installed in rigid conduit, the possibility of damage to the cable as it is pulled through the conduit will be reduced by

choices.A: dusting the cable with powdered graphite.
choices.B: dusting the cable with powdered soapstone.
choices.C: applying a light coat of dielectric grease.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Why are the iron cores of most induction coils laminated?

choices.A: To reduce the core reluctance.
choices.B: To increase the core permeability.
choices.C: To reduce the effects of eddy currents.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: When DC generators are operated in parallel to supply power for a single load, their controls include an equalizer circuit to assure that all generators share the load equally. The equalizer circuit operates by

choices.A: increasing the output of the low generator to equal the output of the high generator.
choices.B: decreasing the output of the high generator to equal the output of the low generator.
choices.C: increasing the output of the low generator and decreasing the output of the high generator until they are equal.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: The poles of a generator are laminated to

choices.A: reduce flux losses.
choices.B: increase flux concentration.
choices.C: reduce eddy current losses.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of the following statements is true?

choices.A: An antiskid system is designed to apply enough force to operate just above the skid point.
choices.B: A warning lamp lights in the flight deck when the antiskid system is turned on.
choices.C: An antiskid system is designed to apply enough force to operate just below the skid point.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: Which of the following are found in a laminated integral electrically heated windshield system?

choices.A: Autotransformer, heat control relay, heat control toggle switch, 115V DC power supply, and indicating light.
choices.B: Heat control relay, heat control toggle switch, 24V DC power supply, and indicating light.
choices.C: Autotransformer, heat control relay, heat control toggle switch, and indicating light.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: What is the principle characteristic of a windshield pneumatic rain removal system?

choices.A: An air blast spreads a liquid rain repellant evenly over the windshield that prevents raindrops from clinging to the glass surface.
choices.B: An air blast forms a barrier that prevents raindrops from striking the windshield surface.
choices.C: A pneumatic rain removal system is simply a mechanical windshield wiper system that is powered by pneumatic system pressure.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: What are the components used to heat water supply lines and waste water tanks?

choices.A: Heater blankets and inline heaters.
choices.B: Combustion heaters and fuel heaters.
choices.C: Heater boots and combustion heaters.

Nonmetallic Structures.question: For doped and overlapping span-wise seams on a wing's leading edge, overlap the fabric at least 4 inches, and

choices.A: cover with finishing tape at least 3 inches wide, with the tape centered on the leading edge of the wing.
choices.B: cover with finishing tape at least 4 inches wide, with the tape centered on the leading edge of the wing.
choices.C: cover with finishing tape at least 4 inches wide, with the tape centered at the outside edge of the overlap seam.

Metallic Structures.question: The strength of a well-designed and properly prepared wood splice joint is provided by the

choices.A: bearing surface of the wood fibers.
choices.B: glue.
choices.C: reinforcement plates.

Metallic Structures.question: Where is information found concerning acceptable species substitutions for wood materials used in aircraft repair?

choices.B: Aircraft Specifications or Type Certificate Data Sheets.
choices.C: Technical Standard Orders.

Metallic Structures.question: In cases of elongated bolt holes in a wood spar or cracks in the vicinity of boltholes,

choices.A: it is permissible to ream the hole, plug with hardwood, and redrill.
choices.B: the spar may be reinforced by using hardwood reinforcing plates.
choices.C: a new section of spar should be spliced in or the spar replaced entirely.

Metallic Structures.question: A faint line running across the grain of a wood spar generally indicates

choices.A: compression failure.
choices.B: shear failure.
choices.C: decay.

Metallic Structures.question: The I-beam wooden spar is routed to

choices.A: increase strength.
choices.B: obtain uniform strength.
choices.C: reduce weight.

Metallic Structures.question: The best method of repair for a fabric-covered surface which has an L-shaped tear, each leg of which is approximately 14 inches long, is to

choices.A: re-cover the entire bay in which the tear is located.
choices.B: sew from the end of each leg to the center of the tear with a baseball stitch and then dope on a patch.
choices.C: sew with a baseball stitch from the center of the tear out toward the extremity of each leg and then dope on a patch.

Metallic Structures.question: Threaded rivets (Rivnuts) are commonly used to

choices.A: join two or more pieces of sheet metal where shear strength is desired.
choices.B: attach parts or components with screws to sheet metal.
choices.C: join two or more pieces of sheet metal where bearing strength is desired.

Metallic Structures.question: What precaution, if any, should be taken to prevent corrosion inside a repaired metal honeycomb structure?

choices.A: Prime the repair with a corrosion inhibitor and seal from the atmosphere.
choices.B: Paint the outside area with several coats of exterior paint.
choices.C: None. Honeycomb is usually made from a man-made or fibrous material which is not susceptible to corrosion.

Metallic Structures.question: A category of plastic material that is capable of softening or flowing when reheated is described as a

choices.A: thermoplastic.
choices.B: thermocure.
choices.C: thermoset.

Metallic Structures.question: A potted compound repair on honeycomb can usually be made on damages less than

choices.A: 4 inches in diameter.
choices.B: 2 inches in diameter.
choices.C: 1 inch in diameter.

Metallic Structures.question: When making repairs to fiberglass, cleaning of the area to be repaired is essential for a good bond. The final cleaning should be made using

choices.A: MEK (methyl ethyl ketone).
choices.B: soap, water, and a scrub brush.
choices.C: a thixotropic agent.

Metallic Structures.question: Fiberglass laminate damage that extends completely through one facing and into the core

choices.A: cannot be repaired.
choices.B: requires the replacement of the damaged core and facing.
choices.C: can be repaired by using a typical metal facing patch.

Metallic Structures.question: The preferred way to make permanent repairs on composites is by

choices.A: bonding on metal or cured composite patches.
choices.B: riveting on metal or cured composite patches.
choices.C: laminating on new repair plies.

Metallic Structures.question: The part of a replacement honeycomb core that must line up with the adjacent original is the

choices.A: cell side.
choices.B: ribbon direction.
choices.C: cell edge.

Metallic Structures.question: If an aircraft's transparent plastic enclosures exhibit fine cracks which may extend in a network over or under the surface or through the plastic, the plastic is said to be

choices.A: hazing.
choices.B: brinelling.
choices.C: crazing.

Metallic Structures.question: When installing transparent plastic enclosures which are retained by bolts extending through the plastic material and self-locking nuts, the nuts should be

choices.A: tightened to a firm fit, plus one full turn.
choices.B: tightened to a firm fit, then backed off one full turn.
choices.C: tightened to a firm fit.

Metallic Structures.question: Which is considered good practice concerning the installation of acrylic plastics?

choices.A: When nuts and bolts are used, the plastic should be installed hot and tightened to a firm fit before the plastic cools.
choices.B: When rivets are used, adequate spacer or other satisfactory means to prevent excessive tightening of the frame to the plastic should be provided.
choices.C: When rivets or nuts and bolts are used, slotted holes are not recommended.

Metallic Structures.question: If no scratches are visible after transparent plastic enclosure materials have been cleaned, their surfaces should be

choices.A: polished with rubbing compound applied with a damp cloth.
choices.B: buffed with a clean, soft, dry cloth.
choices.C: covered with a thin coat of wax.

Metallic Structures.question: Cabin upholstery materials installed in current standard category airplanes must

choices.A: be fireproof.
choices.B: be at least flame resistant.
choices.C: meet the requirements prescribed in Part 43.

Metallic Structures.question: What is the most common method of cementing transparent plastics?

choices.A: Heat method.
choices.B: Soak method.
choices.C: Bevel method.

Metallic Structures.question: What is the purpose of a gusset or gusset plate used in the construction and repair of aircraft structures?

choices.A: To hold structural members in position temporarily until the permanent attachment has been completed.
choices.B: To provide access for inspection of structural attachments.
choices.C: To join and reinforce intersecting structural members.

Metallic Structures.question: Select the alternative which best describes the function of the flute section of a twist drill.

choices.A: Provides a method for cooling oil to be delivered to the cutting surface.
choices.B: Forms the area where the drill bit attaches to the drill motor.
choices.C: Forms the cutting edges of the drill point.

Metallic Structures.question: On a semi-monocoque fuselage, the skin is reinforced by longitudinal structural members called

choices.A: beams and struts.
choices.B: longerons and stringers.
choices.C: formers and bulkheads.

Metallic Structures.question: Shallow scratches in sheet metal may be repaired by

choices.A: burnishing.
choices.B: buffing.
choices.C: stop drilling.

Metallic Structures.question: A single-lap sheet splice is to be used to repair a section of damaged aluminum skin. If a double row of 1/8-inch rivets is used, the minimum allowable overlap will be

choices.A: 1/2 inch.
choices.B: 3/4 inch.
choices.C: 13/16 inch.

Metallic Structures.question: What is the minimum edge distance allowed for aluminum alloy single lap sheet splices containing a single row of rivets as compared to a joint with multiple rows, all rivets being equal in diameter?

choices.A: The minimum edge distance for the single row is greater than that for the multiple row.
choices.B: The minimum edge distance for the single row is less than that for the multiple row.
choices.C: The minimum edge distance for the single row is equal to that for the multiple row.

Metallic Structures.question: Which statement is true regarding the inspection of a stressed skin metal wing assembly known to have been critically loaded?

choices.A: If rivets show no visible distortion, further investigation is unnecessary.
choices.B: If bearing failure has occurred, the rivet shanks will be joggled.
choices.C: If genuine rivet tipping has occurred, groups of consecutive rivet heads will be tipped in the same direction.

Metallic Structures.question: When repairing a small hole on a metal stressed skin, the major consideration in the design of the patch should be

choices.A: the shear strength of the riveted joint.
choices.B: to use rivet spacing similar to a seam in the skin.
choices.C: that the bond between the patch and the skin is sufficient to prevent dissimilar metal corrosion.

Metallic Structures.question: Repairs or splices involving stringers on the lower surface of stressed skin metal wings are usually

choices.A: not permitted.
choices.B: permitted only if the damage does not exceed 6 inches in any direction.
choices.C: permitted but are normally more critical in reference to strength in tension than similar repairs to the upper surface.

Metallic Structures.question: Aircraft structural units, such as spars, engine supports, etc., which have been built up from sheet metal, are normally

choices.A: repairable, using approved methods.
choices.B: repairable, except when subjected to compressive loads.
choices.C: not repairable, but must be replaced when damaged or deteriorated.

Metallic Structures.question: The monocoque fuselage relies largely on the strength of

choices.A: longerons and formers.
choices.B: skin or covering.
choices.C: bulkheads and longerons.

Metallic Structures.question: Which parts of a semi-monocoque fuselage prevent tension and compression from bending the fuselage?

choices.A: Ribs and formers.
choices.B: Longerons and stringers.
choices.C: Bulkheads and skin.

Metallic Structures.question: Heat-treated rivets in the D and DD series that are not driven within the prescribed time after heat treatment or removal from refrigeration

choices.A: must be reheat treated before use.
choices.B: must be discarded.
choices.C: may be returned to refrigeration and used later without reheat treatment.

Metallic Structures.question: A sheet metal repair is to be made using two pieces of 0.040-inch aluminum riveted together. All rivet holes are drilled for 3/32-inch rivets. The length of the rivets to be used will be

choices.A: 1/8 inch.
choices.B: 1/4 inch.
choices.C: 5/16 inch.

Metallic Structures.question: A sheet metal repair is to be made using two pieces of 0.0625-inch aluminum riveted together. All rivet holes are drilled for 1/8-inch rivets. The length of the rivets to be used will be

choices.A: 5/32 inch.
choices.B: 3/16 inch.
choices.C: 5/16 inch.

Metallic Structures.question: Joggles in removed rivet shanks would indicate partial

choices.A: bearing failure.
choices.B: torsion failure.
choices.C: shear failure.

Metallic Structures.question: The length of rivet to be chosen when making a structural repair that involves the joining of 0.032-inch and 0.064-inch aluminum sheet, drilled with a No. 30 drill, is

choices.A: 7/16 inch.
choices.B: 5/16 inch.
choices.C: 1/4 inch.

Metallic Structures.question: If a streamline cover plate is to be hand formed using a form block, a piece of dead soft aluminum should first be placed over the hollow portion of the mold and securely fastened in place. The bumping operation should be

choices.A: distributed evenly over the face of the aluminum at all times rather than being started at the edges or center.
choices.B: started by tapping the aluminum lightly around the edges and gradually working down into the center.
choices.C: started by tapping the aluminum in the center until it touches the bottom of the mold and then working out in all directions.

Metallic Structures.question: The flat layout or blank length of a piece of metal from which a simple L-shaped bracket 3 inches by 1 inch is to be bent depends upon the radius of the desired bend. The bracket which will require the greatest amount of material is one which has a bend radius of

choices.A: 1/8 inch.
choices.B: 1/2 inch.
choices.C: 1/4 inch.

Metallic Structures.question: A piece of sheet metal is bent to a certain radius. The curvature of the bend is referred to as the

choices.A: bend allowance.
choices.B: neutral line.
choices.C: bend radius.

Metallic Structures.question: The purpose of a joggle is to

choices.A: allow clearance for a sheet or an extrusion.
choices.B: increase obstruction for a sheet or an extrusion.
choices.C: decrease the weight of the part and still retain the necessary strength.

Metallic Structures.question: The sight line on a sheet metal flat layout to be bent in a cornice or box brake is measured and marked

choices.A: one-half radius from either bend tangent line.
choices.B: one radius from either bend tangent line.
choices.C: one radius from the bend tangent line that is placed under the brake.

Metallic Structures.question: On a sheet metal fitting layout with a single bend, allow for stretching by

choices.A: adding the setback to each leg.
choices.B: subtracting the setback from one leg.
choices.C: subtracting the setback from both legs.

Metallic Structures.question: Which statement best describes magnesium welding?

choices.A: Magnesium can be welded to other metals.
choices.B: Filler rod should be nickel-steel.
choices.C: Filler rod should be the same composition as base metal.

Metallic Structures.question: Which statement best describes magnesium welding?

choices.A: It has low thermal conductivity, so distortion and cracking rarely occur.
choices.B: It is recommended to use larger welding beads and a slower welding speed than normal.
choices.C: It can be welded successfully using the same type of joints that are used for aluminum.

Metallic Structures.question: Which statement is true in regard to welding heat-treated magnesium?

choices.A: The welded section does not have the strength of the original metal.
choices.B: Flux should not be used because it is very difficult to remove and is likely to cause corrosion.
choices.C: Magnesium cannot be repaired by fusion welding because of the high probability of igniting the metal.

Metallic Structures.question: Engine mount members should preferably be repaired by using a

choices.A: larger diameter tube with fishmouth and no rosette welds.
choices.B: larger diameter tube with fishmouth and rosette welds.
choices.C: smaller diameter tube with fishmouth and rosette welds.

Metallic Structures.question: What method of repair is recommended for a steel tube longeron dented at a cluster?

choices.A: Welded split sleeve.
choices.B: Welded outer sleeve.
choices.C: Welded patch plate.

Metallic Structures.question: What purpose does flux serve in welding aluminum?

choices.A: Removes dirt, grease, and oil.
choices.B: Minimizes or prevents oxidation.
choices.C: Ensures proper distribution of the filler rod.

Metallic Structures.question: Why are aluminum plates 1/4 inch or more thick usually preheated before welding?

choices.A: Reduces internal stresses and assures more complete penetration.
choices.B: Reduces welding time.
choices.C: Prevents corrosion and ensures proper distribution of flux.

Metallic Structures.question: The correct dihedral angle can be determined by

choices.A: measuring the angular setting of each wing at the rear spar with a bubble protractor.
choices.B: placing a straightedge and bubble protractor across the spars while the airplane is in flying position.
choices.C: using a dihedral board and bubble level along the front spar of each wing.

Metallic Structures.question: The dihedral angle of a wing may be measured by placing a straightedge and level protractor on the

choices.A: front spar.
choices.B: wing root.
choices.C: wing chord.

Metallic Structures.question: Proper wing twist in a sheet metal constructed wing can usually be checked by utilizing a

choices.A: plum bob, string, and straightedge.
choices.B: bubble level and special fixtures described by the manufacturer.
choices.C: straightedge, tape measure, and carpenter's square.

Metallic Structures.question: Very often, repairs to a control surface require static rebalancing of the control surface. Generally, flight control balance condition may be determined by

choices.A: checking for equal distribution of weight throughout the control surface.
choices.B: the behavior of the trailing edge when the surface is suspended from its hinge points.
choices.C: suspending the control surface from its leading edge in the stream line position and checking weight distribution.

Metallic Structures.question: Airworthiness Directives (ADs) are designed to notify the

choices.A: mechanic of an approved alternate method to perform a maintenance task other than specified in the aircraft maintenance manual.
choices.B: aircraft owners and operators of methods, techniques, and acceptable practices for inspection and alterations of aircraft.
choices.C: aircraft owners and other interested persons of unsafe conditions and prescribes the condition under which the product may continue to be operated.

Metallic Structures.question: Radio equipment installations made in accordance with Supplemental Type Certificate data require approval for return to service

choices.A: by a field approval from the FAA.
choices.B: by an airframe and powerplant mechanic.
choices.C: by the holder of an inspection authorization.

Metallic Structures.question: For an individual (not a repair station) to conduct a complete 100-hour inspection on an aircraft and approve it for return to service requires a mechanic certificate with

choices.A: an airframe rating only.
choices.B: airframe and powerplant ratings.
choices.C: airframe and powerplant ratings with an inspection authorization.

Metallic Structures.question: The primary purpose for balancing aircraft wheel assemblies is to

choices.A: prevent heavy spots and reduce vibration.
choices.B: distribute the aircraft weight properly.
choices.C: reduce excessive wear and turbulence.

Metallic Structures.question: The operation of an aircraft combustion heater is usually controlled by a thermostat circuit which

choices.A: alternately turns the fuel on and off, a process known as cycling.
choices.B: meters the amount of fuel continuously entering the heater and therefore regulates the heater's BTU output.
choices.C: regulates the voltage applied to the heater's ignition transformer.

Metallic Structures.question: Before a high-pressure oxygen cylinder is serviced, it must be the correct type and have been

choices.A: hydrostatically tested within the proper time interval.
choices.B: approved by the National Transportation Safety Board.
choices.C: inspected by a certificated airframe mechanic.

Metallic Structures.question: Which of the following are characteristics of a chemical or solid-state oxygen system?

choices.A: A volume storage capacity about three times that of compressed oxygen and system generators that are inert below 400F, even under severe impact.
choices.B: An adjustable oxygen release rate and a volume storage capacity about three times that of compressed oxygen.
choices.C: System generators are inert below 400F, even under severe impact, and a distribution and regulating system similar to gaseous oxygen systems.

Metallic Structures.question: Aircraft temperature thermocouple leads may

choices.A: not be altered and are designed for a specific installation.
choices.B: be installed with either lead to either post of the indicator.
choices.C: be repaired using solderless connectors.

Metallic Structures.question: Who is authorized to repair an aircraft instrument?

choices.A: A certified mechanic with an airframe rating.
choices.B: A certificated airframe repair station.
choices.C: A certificated repair station approved for that class instrument.

Metallic Structures.question: A certificated mechanic with at least an airframe rating may perform

choices.A: minor internal repairs to aircraft instruments.
choices.B: major repairs to aircraft instruments.
choices.C: inspections and function checks on aircraft instruments.

Metallic Structures.question: A certificated mechanic with airframe and powerplant ratings may

choices.A: perform minor repairs to aircraft instruments.
choices.B: perform minor repairs and minor alterations to aircraft instruments.
choices.C: not perform repairs to aircraft instruments.

Metallic Structures.question: An emergency locator transmitter (ELT) is normally activated by an inertial switch or equivalent mechanism if subjected to a force of a prescribed intensity and duration. It must activate when the force is applied

choices.A: parallel to the longitudinal axis of the aircraft.
choices.B: parallel to the vertical axis of the aircraft.
choices.C: in any direction relative to the aircraft axes.

Metallic Structures.question: Aircraft antenna must be grounded to the

choices.A: airframe.
choices.B: wing spar.
choices.C: bus bar.

Metallic Structures.question: When bending coaxial cable, the bend radius should be at least

choices.A: 10 times the diameter of the cable.
choices.B: 15 times the diameter of the cable.
choices.C: 20 times the diameter of the cable.

Metallic Structures.question: Which of the following may be used for the repair of fuel leaks on most integral fuel tanks?

choices.A: Welding and resealing.
choices.B: Brazing and resealing.
choices.C: Riveting and resealing.

Metallic Structures.question: What should be used to inert an integral fuel tank before attempting repairs?

choices.B: Water.
choices.C: Steam.

Metallic Structures.question: What should be used to remove flux from an aluminum tank after welded repairs?

choices.A: Soft brush and warm water.
choices.B: Five percent solution of nitric or sulfuric acid.
choices.C: Mild solution of soap and warm water.

Metallic Structures.question: Which of the following is necessary to effectively troubleshoot a fuel pressure warning system?

choices.A: The manufacturer's maintenance manuals.
choices.B: AC 43.13-1, Acceptable Methods, Techniques, and Practices -- Aircraft Inspection and Repair.
choices.C: A set of Federal Aviation Regulations.

Metallic Structures.question: Which of the following would be most useful to locate and troubleshoot an internal fuel leak in an aircraft fuel system?

choices.A: Structural repair manual.
choices.B: Orthographic projection troubleshooting tree.
choices.C: Maintenance manual diagrams and descriptions.

Metallic Structures.question: In a generator, what eliminates any possible sparking to the brush guides caused by the movement of the brushes within the holder?

choices.A: The brush pigtail.
choices.B: Brush spring tension.
choices.C: Undercutting the mica on the commutator.

Metallic Structures.question: How can it be determined if a transformer winding has some of its turns shorted together?

choices.A: Measure the input voltage with an ohmmeter.
choices.B: The output voltage will be high.
choices.C: The transformer will get hot in normal operation.

Metallic Structures.question: Which of the following are the major parts of a DC motor?

choices.A: Armature assembly, field assembly, brush assembly, and end frame.
choices.B: Field assembly, brush assembly, commutator, and pole piece.
choices.C: Brush assembly, pole piece, rheostat, and end frame.

Metallic Structures.question: What is the appropriate method for installing pre-insulated terminal lugs and splices to electric wires?

choices.A: Using a crimping tool to secure the terminal lug.
choices.B: Soldering the terminal lug to the end of the wire.
choices.C: Soldering the exposed wire prior to crimping on the terminal lug.

Metallic Structures.question: How should the splices be arranged if several are to be located in an electrical wire bundle?

choices.A: Staggered along the length of the bundle.
choices.B: Grouped together to facilitate inspection.
choices.C: Splicing within wire bundles is not permitted.

Metallic Structures.question: What is the minimum bend radius for an electrical wire bundle?

choices.A: Ten times the outside diameter of the bundle.
choices.B: Five times the outside diameter of the bundle.
choices.C: Fifteen times the outside diameter of the bundle.

Metallic Structures.question: When approved, splices may be used to repair manufactured harnesses or installed wiring. The maximum number of splices permitted between any two connectors is

choices.A: one.
choices.B: two.
choices.C: three.

Metallic Structures.question: Electric wiring installed in aircraft without special enclosing means (open wiring) offers the advantages of ease of installation, simple maintenance, and reduced weight. When bundling open wiring, the bundles should

choices.A: be limited as to the number of cables to minimize damage from a single electrical fault.
choices.B: include at least one shielded cable to provide good bonding of the bundle to the airframe.
choices.C: be limited to a minimum bend radius of five times the bundle diameter to avoid excessive stresses on the cable insulation.

Metallic Structures.question: How should a coaxial cable be routed?

choices.A: Parallel with stringers or ribs.
choices.B: Perpendicular to stringers or ribs.
choices.C: As directly as possible.

Metallic Structures.question: Which statement relating to electric wiring is true?

choices.A: When attaching a terminal to the end of an electric cable, it should be determined that the strength of the cable-to-terminal joint is at least twice the tensile strength of the cable.
choices.B: When attaching a terminal to the end of an electric cable, it should be determined that the strength of the cable-to-terminal joint is at least equal to the tensile strength of the cable itself.
choices.C: All electric cable splices should be covered with soft insulating tubing (spaghetti) for mechanical protection against external abrasion.

Metallic Structures.question: What should be used to protect wires from chafing when they must pass through bulkheads, firewalls, ribs, etc.?

choices.A: Aerodynamic duct tape.
choices.B: Suitable grommet.
choices.C: Plastic spiral wrap.

Metallic Structures.question: A certain switch is described as a single-pole, double-throw (SPDT) switch. The throw of a switch indicates the number of

choices.A: circuits each pole can complete through the switch.
choices.B: terminals at which current can enter or leave the switch.
choices.C: places at which the operating device (toggle, plunger, etc.) will come to rest and at the same time open or close a circuit.

Metallic Structures.question: The voltage in an AC transformer secondary that contains twice as many loops as the primary will be

choices.A: greater and the amperage less than in the primary.
choices.B: greater and the amperage greater than in the primary.
choices.C: less and the amperage greater than in the primary.

Metallic Structures.question: The major advantages of alternating current (AC) over direct current (DC) is the fact that its current and voltage can easily be increased or decreased

choices.A: by means of a inverter.
choices.B: by means of a rectifier.
choices.C: by means of a transformer.

Metallic Structures.question: What is the ratio of turns between the primary coil winding and the secondary coil winding of a transformer designed to triple its input voltage?

choices.A: Primary will have one-third as many turns as its secondary.
choices.B: Primary will have twice as many turns as its secondary.
choices.C: Primary will have three times as many turns as its secondary.

Metallic Structures.question: Some aircraft are protected against airframe icing by heating the leading edges of the airfoils and intake ducts. When is this type of anti-ice system usually operated during flight?

choices.A: Continuously while the aircraft is in flight.
choices.B: In symmetric cycles during icing conditions to remove ice as it accumulates.
choices.C: Whenever icing conditions are first encountered or expected to occur.

Metallic Structures.question: Where are the heating elements located on most aircraft with electrically heated windshields?

choices.A: Laminated in the glass.
choices.B: Attached to the glass.
choices.C: Inside the windshield frame.

Metallic Structures.question: Arcing in an electrically heated windshield panel usually indicates a breakdown in the

choices.A: temperature-sensing elements.
choices.B: autotransformers.
choices.C: conductive coating.

Metallic Structures.question: What are three methods of anti-icing aircraft windshields?

choices.A: An electric heating element in the windshield, a heated air circulating system, and windshield wipers and anti-icing fluid.
choices.B: A blanket-type heating system, an electric heating element in the windshield, and a ribbon-type heating system.
choices.C: An electric heating element in the windshield, a heated air circulating system, and a hot water system.

Metallic Structures.question: Maintenance of fire-detection systems includes the

choices.A: repair of damaged sensing elements.
choices.B: servicing pressure type responder system.
choices.C: replacement of damaged sensing elements.

Metallic Structures.question: Which type of monocoque structure has the skin reinforced by a complete framework of structural members?

choices.A: Semimonocoque.
choices.B: Reinforced shell.
choices.C: Monocoque.

Landing Gear.question: The cantilever wing uses

choices.A: external struts or wire bracing.
choices.B: no external bracing.
choices.C: the skin to carry most of the load to the wing butt.

Landing Gear.question: Which of the following can normally be welded without adversely affecting strength?

choices.A: Spring steel struts or aircraft bolts.
choices.B: Most heat-treated steel/nickel alloy components.
choices.C: SAE 4130 chrome/molybdenum tubing.

Landing Gear.question: In brake service work, the term 'bleeding brakes' is the process of

choices.A: withdrawing air only from the system.
choices.B: withdrawing fluid from the system for the purpose of removing air that has entered the system.
choices.C: replacing small amounts of fluid in reservoir.

Landing Gear.question: To prevent a very rapid extension of an oleo shock strut after initial compression resulting from landing impact,

choices.A: various types of valves or orifices are used which restrict the reverse fluid flow.
choices.B: the metering pin gradually reduces the size of the orifice as the shock strut extends.
choices.C: the air is forced through a restricted orifice in the reverse direction.

Landing Gear.question: A pilot reports the right brake on an aircraft is spongy when the brake pedal is depressed in a normal manner. The probable cause is

choices.A: the hydraulic master cylinder piston is sticking.
choices.B: air in the brake hydraulic system.
choices.C: the hydraulic master cylinder piston return spring is weak.

Landing Gear.question: Why do most aircraft tire manufacturers recommend that the tubes in newly installed tires be first inflated, fully deflated, and then reinflated to the correct pressure?

choices.A: To allow the tube to position itself correctly inside the tire.
choices.B: To eliminate all the air between the tube and the inside of the tire.
choices.C: To test the entire assembly for leaks.

Landing Gear.question: The metering pins in oleo shock struts serve to

choices.A: lock the struts in the DOWN position.
choices.B: retard the flow of oil as the struts are compressed.
choices.C: meter the proper amount of air in the struts.

Landing Gear.question: What action should be taken whenever maintenance is performed that will affect the landing gear system performance?

choices.A: A test flight should be performed to conduct an operational check.
choices.B: A technician with an inspection authorization should perform a visual inspection.
choices.C: The aircraft should be placed on jacks and a retraction test should be performed.

Landing Gear.question: When working with high pressure, high performance tires, why is it recommended to deflate the tires when removing wheels from the axle?

choices.A: To relieve the strain on the wheel retaining nut and axle threads.
choices.B: To ensure safety in case of a defective wheel or broken tie bolts.
choices.C: To reduce the size of the tire for ease of removal.

Landing Gear.question: The braking action of a Cleveland disc brake is accomplished by compressing a rotating brake disc between two opposite brake linings. How is equal pressure on both sides of the rotating disc assured?

choices.A: By allowing the brake rotor to float to automatically equalize as pressure is applied to the rotor.
choices.B: By allowing the caliper to float to automatically equalize as pressure is applied to the rotor.
choices.C: By allowing the brake linings to automatically equalize as pressure is applied to the rotor.

Landing Gear.question: If it is determined that spongy brake action is not caused by air in the brake system, what is the next most likely cause?

choices.A: Worn brake lining.
choices.B: Internal leakage in the master cylinder.
choices.C: Deteriorated flexible hoses.

Landing Gear.question: A brake debooster valve is installed in systems where the high pressure of the hydraulic system (3,000 psi) is used to operate brakes

choices.A: that are designed to work with lower pressure.
choices.B: that are used in conjunction with an antiskid system.
choices.C: that are used on aircraft having high landing speeds.

Landing Gear.question: A stripe or mark applied to a wheel rim and extending onto the sidewall of a tube-type tire is a

choices.A: slippage mark.
choices.B: wheel-to-tire balance mark.
choices.C: wheel weight reference mark.

Landing Gear.question: Overinflated aircraft tires may cause damage to the

choices.A: brake linings.
choices.B: wheel hub.
choices.C: wheel flange.

Landing Gear.question: Debooster valves are used in brake systems primarily to

choices.A: ensure rapid application and release of the brakes.
choices.B: reduce brake pressure and maintain static pressure.
choices.C: reduce the pressure and release the brakes rapidly.

Landing Gear.question: The repair for an out-of-tolerance toe-in condition of main landing gear wheels determined not to be the result of bent or twisted components consists of

choices.A: shimming the axle in the oleo trunnion.
choices.B: inserting, removing, or changing the location of washers or spacers at the center pivotal point of the scissor torque links.
choices.C: placing shims or spacers behind the bearing of the out-of-tolerance wheel or wheels.

Landing Gear.question: Power boost brake systems are used on aircraft that have

choices.A: high landing speeds.
choices.B: low normal hydraulic system pressure.
choices.C: more than one brake assembly per axle.

Landing Gear.question: What is the purpose of the torque links attached to the cylinder and piston of a landing gear oleo strut?

choices.A: Limit compression stroke.
choices.B: Hold the strut in place.
choices.C: Maintain correct wheel alignment.

Landing Gear.question: The removal, installation, and repair of landing gear tires by the holder of a private pilot certificate on an aircraft owned or operated is considered to be

choices.A: a violation of the Federal Aviation Regulations.
choices.B: a minor repair.
choices.C: preventive maintenance.

Landing Gear.question: Aircraft brakes requiring a large volume of fluid to operate the brakes generally

choices.A: use independent master cylinder systems.
choices.B: do not use brake system accumulators.
choices.C: use power brake control valves.

Landing Gear.question: What is one effect a restricted compensator port of a master cylinder will have on a brake system?

choices.A: The brakes will operate normally.
choices.B: The reservoir will be filled by reverse flow.
choices.C: The restriction will cause slow release of the brakes.

Landing Gear.question: When an air/oil type of landing gear shock strut is used, the initial shock of landing is cushioned by

choices.A: compression of the air charge.
choices.B: the fluid being forced through a metered opening.
choices.C: compression of the fluid.

Landing Gear.question: Internal leakage in a brake master cylinder unit can cause

choices.A: fading brakes.
choices.B: slow release of brakes.
choices.C: the pedal to slowly creep down while pedal pressure is applied.

Landing Gear.question: A sleeve, spacer, or bumper ring is incorporated in a landing gear oleo shock strut

choices.A: limit the extension of the torque arm.
choices.B: limit the extension stroke.
choices.C: reduce the rebound effect.

Landing Gear.question: Which statement is true with respect to an aircraft equipped with hydraulically operated multiple-disc type brake assemblies?

choices.A: There are no minimum or maximum disc clearance checks required due to the use of self-compensating cylinder assemblies.
choices.B: Do not set parking brake when brakes are hot.
choices.C: No parking brake provisions are possible for this type of brake assembly.

Landing Gear.question: When servicing an air/oil shock strut with MIL-5606 the strut should be

choices.A: collapsed and fluid added at the filler opening.
choices.B: fully extended and fluid added at the filler opening.
choices.C: partially extended and fluid added at the filler opening.

Landing Gear.question: Instructions concerning the type of fluid and amount of air pressure to be put in a shock strut are found

choices.A: on the airplane data plate.
choices.B: in the aircraft operations limitations.
choices.C: in the aircraft manufacturer's service manual.

Landing Gear.question: The purpose of a relief valve in a brake system is to

choices.A: reduce pressure for brake application.
choices.B: prevent the tire from skidding.
choices.C: compensate for thermal expansion.

Landing Gear.question: Aircraft tire pressure should be checked

choices.A: using only a push on stick-type gauge having one-pound increments.
choices.B: at least once a week or more often.
choices.C: as soon as possible after each flight.

Landing Gear.question: If the extended longitudinal axis of the main landing gear wheel assemblies intersects aft of the aircraft, the wheels can be termed as having

choices.A: toe-out.
choices.B: toe-in.
choices.C: negative camber.

Landing Gear.question: What is the purpose of a compensating port or valve in a brake master cylinder of an independent brake system?

choices.A: Permits the fluid to flow toward or away from the reservoir as temperature changes.
choices.B: Assists in the master cylinder piston return.
choices.C: Prevents fluid from flowing back to the reservoir.

Landing Gear.question: If an aircraft shock strut (air/oil type) bottoms upon initial landing contact, but functions correctly during taxi, the most probable cause is

choices.A: low fluid.
choices.B: low air charge.
choices.C: a restricted metering pin orifice.

Landing Gear.question: What is the function of a cam incorporated in a nose gear shock strut?

choices.A: Provides an internal shimmy damper.
choices.B: Straightens the nosewheel.
choices.C: Provides steering of aircraft during ground operation.

Landing Gear.question: Extension of an oleo shock strut is measured to determine the

choices.A: amount of oil in the strut.
choices.B: physical condition of the strut itself.
choices.C: proper operating position of the strut.

Landing Gear.question: Debooster cylinders are used in brake systems primarily to

choices.A: reduce brake pressure and maintain static pressure.
choices.B: relieve excessive fluid and ensure a positive release.
choices.C: reduce the pressure to the brake and increase the volume of fluid flow.

Landing Gear.question: If a shock strut bottoms after it has been properly serviced, the

choices.A: strut should be disassembled and the metering pin orifice plate replaced.
choices.B: air pressure should be increased.
choices.C: strut should be removed, disassembled, and inspected.

Landing Gear.question: A high-speed aircraft tire with a sound cord body and bead may be recapped

choices.A: a maximum of three times.
choices.B: only by the tire manufacturer.
choices.C: an indefinite number of times.

Landing Gear.question: If an airplane equipped with master cylinders and single-disc brakes has excessive brake pedal travel, but the brakes are hard and effective, the probable cause is

choices.A: the master cylinder one-way cup is leaking.
choices.B: worn brake linings.
choices.C: worn brake disc causing excessive clearance between the notches on the perimeter of the disc and the splines or keys on the wheel.

Landing Gear.question: The correct inflation pressure for an aircraft tire can be obtained from

choices.A: tire manufacturer's specifications.
choices.B: the aircraft service manual.

Landing Gear.question: What should be checked when a shock strut bottoms during a landing?

choices.A: Air pressure.
choices.B: Packing seals for correct installation.
choices.C: Fluid level.

Landing Gear.question: The hydraulic packing seals used in a landing gear shock strut are

choices.A: generally designed to be compatible with more than one type of fluid.
choices.B: kept from direct contact with fluid by teflon or nylon backup rings.
choices.C: used only with a specific type of fluid.

Landing Gear.question: A close inspection of a fusible plug reveals the core has experienced some deformation. What is the appropriate maintenance procedure?

choices.A: Replace all the fusible plugs.
choices.B: Replace the affected fusible plugs.
choices.C: Replace with new wheel assembly.

Landing Gear.question: Chines are used on some aircraft nose wheel tires to help

choices.A: nose gear extension at higher air speeds.
choices.B: reduce the possibility of hydroplaning.

Landing Gear.question: The best safeguards against heat buildup in aircraft tires are

choices.A: proper tire inflation, minimum braking, and ground rolls into the wind.
choices.B: short ground rolls, slow taxi speeds, minimum braking, and proper tire inflation.
choices.C: minimum braking, proper tire inflation, and long ground rolls.

Landing Gear.question: The fusible plugs installed in some aircraft wheels will

choices.A: indicate tire tread separation.
choices.B: prevent overinflation.

Landing Gear.question: What action, if any, should be taken when there is a difference of more than 5 pounds of air pressure in tires mounted as duals?

choices.A: Replace both tires.
choices.B: Correct the discrepancy and enter in the aircraft records.
choices.C: Replace the tire with the lowest pressure.

Landing Gear.question: How long should the aviation mechanic wait after a flight before checking tire pressure?

choices.A: At least 2 hours (3 hours in hot weather).
choices.B: At least 3 hours (4 hours in hot weather).
choices.C: At least 4 hours (5 hours in hot weather).

Landing Gear.question: What is the minimum amount of time to wait for tires to cool before checking tire pressure in hot weather?

choices.A: 3 hours.
choices.B: 2 hours.
choices.C: 1 hour.

Landing Gear.question: Excessive wear in the shoulder area of an aircraft tire is an indication of

choices.A: overinflation.
choices.B: excessive toe-in.
choices.C: underinflation.

Landing Gear.question: Excessive wear in the center of the tread of an aircraft tire is an indication of

choices.A: misalignment.
choices.B: underinflation.
choices.C: overinflation.

Landing Gear.question: When servicing an empty shock strut with fluid, the strut should be completely compressed and extended several times to ensure

choices.A: the piston rod and wiper are lubricated with hydraulic fluid.
choices.B: all excess hydraulic fluid is forced out of the strut.
choices.C: proper packing ring seating and removal of air bubbles.

Landing Gear.question: In shock struts, chevron seals are used to

choices.A: absorb bottoming effect.
choices.B: prevent oil from escaping.
choices.C: serve as a bearing surface.

Landing Gear.question: On most aircraft, the oil level of an air and oil shock strut is checked by

choices.A: removing the oil filler plug and inserting a gauge.
choices.B: measuring the length of the strut extension with a certain air pressure in the strut.
choices.C: releasing the air and seeing that the oil is to the level of the filler plug.

Landing Gear.question: An electric motor used to raise and lower a landing gear would most likely be a

choices.A: shunt field series-wound motor.
choices.B: split field shunt-wound motor.
choices.C: split field series-wound motor.

Landing Gear.question: Nose gear centering cams are used in many retractable landing gear systems. The primary purpose of the centering device is to

choices.A: align the nosewheel prior to touchdown.
choices.B: engage the nosewheel steering.
choices.C: center the nosewheel before it enters the wheel well.

Landing Gear.question: A special bolt in a landing gear attachment requires a torque value of 440 inch-pounds. How many foot-pounds are required?
To be eligible for recharging, a DOT 3HT oxygen cylinder must have been hydrostatically tested every three years and be retired from service after

choices.A: 24 years or 4,380 filling cycles.
choices.B: 15 years or 10,000 filling cycles.
choices.C: 10 years or 5,000 filling cycles.

Landing Gear.question: If it is necessary to enter an aircraft's fuel tank, which procedure should be avoided?

choices.A: Continue purging the tank during the entire work period.
choices.B: Station an assistant outside the fuel tank access to perform rescue operations if required.
choices.C: Conduct the defueling and tank purging operation in an air-conditioned building.

Landing Gear.question: Which motor would be most likely to have an armature brake?

choices.A: Starter motor.
choices.B: Landing light retraction motor.

Landing Gear.question: How does the magnetic brake used to stop rotation of an electric motor armature operate?

choices.A: Centrifugal force releases a rotating brake cog from a stationary notch when the armature reaches a certain speed and magnetic force re-engages the cog when the electrical power is turned off.
choices.B: A friction brake is applied by a magnet and released by a spring.
choices.C: A friction brake is applied by a spring and released by a magnet.

Landing Gear.question: An antiskid system is designed to

choices.A: solely sense the deceleration rate of every main landing gear wheel.
choices.B: release then reapply pressure at a slightly lower value only when a skid is detected.
choices.C: sense the deceleration rate of every main landing gear wheel and release then reapply pressure at a slightly lower value when a skid is detected.

Landing Gear.question: In a brake antiskid system, when an approaching skid is sensed, an electrical signal is sent to the skid control valve which

choices.A: acts as a bypass for the debooster cylinders.
choices.B: relieves the hydraulic pressure on the brake.
choices.C: equalizes the hydraulic pressure in adjacent brakes.

Landing Gear.question: An antiskid system is

choices.A: a hydraulic system.
choices.B: an electrohydraulic system.
choices.C: an electrical system.

Landing Gear.question: Antiskid braking systems are generally armed by

choices.A: the application of brakes.
choices.B: a switch in the cockpit.
choices.C: the rotation of the wheels above a certain speed.

Landing Gear.question: Which of the following statements is true?

choices.A: When an airplane is slowed below approximately 20 mph, the antiskid system automatically activates to give the pilot full control of the brakes for maneuvering and parking.
choices.B: An antiskid system consists of two components; wheel speed sensors and control valves.
choices.C: When an airplane is slowed below approximately 20 mph, the antiskid system automatically deactivates to give the pilot full control of the brakes for maneuvering and parking.

Landing Gear.question: In an antiskid system, wheel skid is detected by

choices.A: an electrical sensor.
choices.B: a discriminator.
choices.C: a sudden rise in brake pressure.

Landing Gear.question: Which of the following functions does a skid control system perform?

choices.A: Normal skid control, normal braking, fail safe protection, locked wheel skid control.
choices.B: Normal skid control, fail safe protection, locked wheel skid control, touchdown protection.
choices.C: Normal skid control, normal braking, touchdown protection, takeoff protection.

Landing Gear.question: In the air with the antiskid armed, current cannot flow to the antiskid control box because the landing gear

choices.A: squat switch is open.
choices.B: down and lock switch is open.

Landing Gear.question: At what point in the landing operation does normal skid control perform its function?

choices.A: When wheel rotation deceleration indicates an impending skid.
choices.B: When wheel rotation indicates hydroplaning condition.
choices.C: Anytime the wheel is rotating.

Landing Gear.question: Which of the following conditions is most likely to cause the landing gear warning signal to sound?

choices.A: Landing gear locked down and throttle advanced.
choices.B: Landing gear locked down and throttle retarded.
choices.C: Landing gear not locked down and throttle retarded.

Landing Gear.question: Where is the landing gear safety switch usually located?

choices.A: On the main gear shock strut.
choices.B: On the landing gear drag brace.
choices.C: On the pilot's control pedestal.

Landing Gear.question: What safety device is actuated by the compression and extension of a landing gear strut?

choices.A: Uplock switch.
choices.B: Downlock switch.
choices.C: Ground safety switch.

Landing Gear.question: Which repair would require a landing gear retraction test?

choices.A: Landing gear safety switch.
choices.B: Red warning light bulb.
choices.C: Gear downlock microswitch.

Landing Gear.question: Landing gear warning systems usually provide which of the following indications?

choices.A: Red light for unsafe gear; green light for gear up.
choices.B: Green light for gear up and down; red light for unsafe gear.
choices.C: Red light for unsafe gear; green light for gear down; no light for gear up.

Landing Gear.question: When a landing gear safety switch on a main gear strut closes at liftoff, which system is deactivated?

choices.A: Landing gear position system.
choices.B: Antiskid system.
choices.C: Aural warning system.

Landing Gear.question: The proper fire-extinguishing agent to use on an aircraft brake fire is

choices.A: water.
choices.B: carbon dioxide.
choices.C: dry powder chemical.

Landing Gear.question: An amber light in a gear position indicator system typically indicates what condition?

choices.A: At least one gear is in transition.
choices.B: The airspeed is too high for selecting gear down.

Flight Controls.question: Which statement is true regarding a cantilever wing?

choices.A: It has nonadjustable lift struts.

choices.C: It requires only one lift strut on each side.

Flight Controls.question: If it is necessary to compute a bend allowance problem and bend allowance tables are not available, the neutral axis of the bend can be

choices.A: represented by the actual length of the required material for the bend.
choices.B: found by adding approximately one-half of the stock thickness to the bend radius.
choices.C: found by subtracting the stock thickness from the bend radius.

Flight Controls.question: Unless otherwise specified, the radius of a bend is the

choices.A: inside radius of the metal being formed.
choices.B: inside radius plus one-half the thickness of the metal being formed.
choices.C: radius of the neutral axis plus one-half the thickness of the metal being formed.

Flight Controls.question: When bending metal, the material on the outside of the curve stretches while the material on the inside of the curve compresses. That part of the material which is not affected by either stress is the

choices.A: mold line.
choices.B: bend tangent line.
choices.C: neutral line.

Flight Controls.question: The oxyacetylene flame for silver soldering should be

choices.A: oxidizing.
choices.B: neutral.
choices.C: carburizing.

Flight Controls.question: The oxyacetylene flame used for aluminum welding should

choices.A: be neutral and soft.
choices.B: be slightly oxidizing.
choices.C: contain an excess of acetylene and leave the tip at a relatively low speed.

Flight Controls.question: Filing or grinding a weld bead is considered to be

choices.A: an acceptable means to achieve a smoother surface.
choices.B: a way to bring about strength reduction in joint.
choices.C: frequently necessary to avoid adding excess weight to a weld joint.

Flight Controls.question: Acetylene at a line pressure above 15 psi is

choices.A: dangerously unstable.
choices.B: used when a reducing flame is necessary.
choices.C: usually necessary when welding metal over 3/8-inch thick.

Flight Controls.question: How should a welding torch flame be adjusted to weld stainless steel?

choices.A: Slightly carburizing.
choices.B: Slightly oxidizing.
choices.C: Neutral.

Flight Controls.question: Wing dihedral, a rigging consideration on most airplanes of conventional design, contributes most to stability of the airplane about its

choices.A: longitudinal axis.
choices.B: vertical axis.
choices.C: lateral axis.

Flight Controls.question: Other than the manufacturer maintenance manual what other document could be used to determine the primary flight control surface deflection for an imported aircraft that is reassembled after shipment?

choices.A: Aircraft Type Certificate Data Sheet.
choices.B: Import manual for the aircraft.

Flight Controls.question: An airplane which has good longitudinal stability should have a minimum tendency to

choices.A: roll.
choices.B: pitch.
choices.C: yaw.

choices.B: a line parallel to the wing chord and a line parallel to the longitudinal axis of the aircraft.

Flight Controls.question: An airplane is controlled directionally about its vertical axis by the

choices.A: rudder.
choices.B: elevator(s).
choices.C: ailerons.

Flight Controls.question: The elevators of a conventional airplane are used to provide rotation about the

choices.A: longitudinal axis.
choices.B: lateral axis.
choices.C: vertical axis.

Flight Controls.question: Washing-in the left wing of a monoplane, for purposes of rigging corrections after flight test, will have what effect on the lift and drag of that wing?

choices.A: lateral axis.
choices.B: longitudinal axis.
choices.C: vertical axis.

Flight Controls.question: An airplane that has a tendency to gradually increase a pitching moment that has been set into motion has

choices.A: poor longitudinal stability.
choices.B: good lateral stability.
choices.C: poor lateral stability.

choices.A: tail into the wind.

choices.A: Check the surface of the swaged portion of the terminal for small cracks which indicate incomplete swaging.

choices.A: no more than four threads are exposed on either side of the turnbuckle barrel.

choices.A: the full rated strength of the cable.
choices.B: 80 percent of the full rated strength of the cable.
choices.C: 70 percent of the full rated strength of the cable.

choices.A: directional stability.
choices.B: longitudinal stability.
choices.C: lateral stability.

Flight Controls.question: How are changes in direction of a control cable accomplished?

choices.A: Pulleys.
choices.B: Bell cranks.
choices.C: Fairleads.

Flight Controls.question: What is the smallest size cable that may be used in aircraft primary control systems?

choices.A: 1/4 inch.
choices.B: 5/16 inch.
choices.C: 1/8 inch.

Flight Controls.question: After repairing or re-covering a rudder, the surface should be rebalanced

choices.A: to its spanwise axis.
choices.B: in its normal flight position.
choices.C: to manufacturer's specifications.

Flight Controls.question: Placing a piece of cloth around a stainless steel control cable and running it back and forth over the length of the cable is generally a satisfactory method of inspecting for

choices.A: corrosion.
