choices.C: excessive wear.

Flight Controls.question: The cable-operated control system of an all-metal aircraft, not incorporating a temperature compensating device, has been rigged to the correct tension in a heated hangar. If the aircraft is operated in very cold weather, the cable tension will

choices.A: decrease when the aircraft structure and cables become cold.
choices.B: increase when the aircraft structure and cables become cold.
choices.C: be unaffected if stainless steel cable is installed.

Flight Controls.question: Excessive wear on both of the sides of a control cable pulley groove is evidence of

choices.A: pulley misalignment.
choices.B: cable misalignment.
choices.C: excessive cable tension.

Flight Controls.question: Fairleads should never deflect the alignment of a cable more than
Where does the breakage of control cable wires occur most frequently?

choices.A: Breakage usually occurs where cables pass over pulleys and through fairleads.
choices.B: Breakage sites are unpredictable and usually occur randomly anywhere along the length of a cable.
choices.C: Breakage usually occurs where cables are swaged to turnbuckle and ball terminals.

Flight Controls.question: With which system is differential control associated?

choices.A: Trim.
choices.B: Aileron.
choices.C: Elevator.

Flight Controls.question: Which statement concerning the 100-hour inspection of an airplane equipped with a push-pull tube-type control system is true?

choices.A: The threaded rod ends should not be adjusted in length for rigging purposes because the rod ends have been properly positioned and staked during manufacture.
choices.B: The terminal end threads of the turnbuckles should be visible through the safety hole in the barrel.
choices.C: The threaded rod ends should be checked for the amount of thread engagement by means of the inspection hole provided.

Flight Controls.question: If control cables are adjusted properly and the control surfaces tend to vibrate, the probable cause is

choices.A: worn attachment fittings.
choices.B: oil can effects on the control surfaces.
choices.C: excessive cable tension.

Flight Controls.question: Aircraft flight control trim systems must be designed and installed so that the

choices.A: pilot can determine the relative position of the trim tab from the cockpit.
choices.B: operating control and the trim tab will always move in the same direction.

Flight Controls.question: Stability about the axis which runs parallel to the line of flight is referred to

choices.A: longitudinal stability.
choices.B: lateral stability.
choices.C: directional stability.

Flight Controls.question: The purpose of spring tabs or servo tabs is to

choices.A: assist the pilot in moving the control surfaces.
choices.B: contribute to the static balance of the control surface.
choices.C: make in-flight trim adjustments possible.

choices.C: down and the elevator will move up.

Flight Controls.question: Movement of the flight deck control toward the nosedown position during a ground operational check of the elevator trim tab system will cause the trailing edge of the trim tab to move in which direction?

choices.A: Downward regardless of elevator position.
choices.B: Upward regardless of elevator position.
choices.C: Downward if the elevator is in the UP position and upward if the elevator is in the DOWN position.

Flight Controls.question: If the control stick of an aircraft with properly rigged flight controls is moved forward and to the right, the left aileron will move

choices.A: up and the elevator will move down.

choices.C: down and the elevator will move down.

Flight Controls.question: If the travel of an airplane's controls is correct but the cables are rigged exceptionally tight, what probable effect will this have when flying the airplane?

choices.A: The airplane will tend to fall off on one wing.
choices.B: The airplane will be heavy on the controls.
choices.C: The pilot will be unable to fly the airplane hands-off.

Flight Controls.question: During inspection of the flight control system of an airplane equipped with differential-type aileron control, side-to-side movement of the control stick will cause

choices.B: each aileron to have greater down travel (from the streamlined position) than up travel.
choices.C: the left aileron to move through a greater number of degrees (from full up to full down) than the right aileron.

Flight Controls.question: A universal propeller protractor used to measure the degrees of aileron travel should be zeroed

choices.A: with the aileron in the NEUTRAL position.
choices.B: with the aileron in the DOWN position.

Flight Controls.question: The universal propeller protractor can be used to measure

choices.A: propeller track.
choices.B: aspect ratio of a wing.
choices.C: degrees of flap travel.

Flight Controls.question: A tension regulator in the flight control cable system of a large all-metal aircraft is used primarily to

choices.A: increase the cable tension in cold weather.
choices.B: provide a means of changing cable tension in flight.
choices.C: retain a set tension.

Flight Controls.question: Differential control on an aileron system means that

choices.A: the down travel is more than the up travel.
choices.B: the up travel is more than the down travel.

Flight Controls.question: What would be the effect if the piston return spring broke in a brake master cylinder?

choices.A: The brakes would become spongy.
choices.B: The brake travel would become excessive.
choices.C: The brakes would drag.

Flight Controls.question: Many brake types can be adapted to operate mechanically or hydraulically. Which type is not adaptable to mechanical operation?

choices.A: Single-disk spot type.
choices.B: Single-servo type.
choices.C: Expander-tube type.

Flight Controls.question: When bleeding aircraft brakes, one of the indications that the air has been purged from the system is

choices.A: partial brake pedal travel.
choices.B: full brake pedal travel.

Flight Controls.question: On all aircraft equipped with retractable landing gear, some means must be provided

choices.A: retract and extend the landing gear if the normal operating mechanism fails.
choices.B: extend the landing gear if the normal operating mechanism fails.

Flight Controls.question: How is it determined in a master cylinder brake system that all of the air has been purged from the system?

Flight Controls.question: Lockout deboosters are primarily pressure reducing valves that

choices.B: cannot allow full debooster piston travel without fluid from the high pressure side entering the low pressure chamber.

Flight Controls.question: A pilot reports that the brake pedals have excessive travel. A probable cause is

choices.A: weak return springs
choices.B: lack of fluid in the brake system.
choices.C: oil or some foreign matter on the brake rotors and linings.

Flight Controls.question: A flexible hydraulic hose identified as MIL-H-8788 will have a stripe running the length of the hose. This stripe is

choices.B: for high pressure fluids with a flexing range.
choices.C: suitable for a wide temperature range.

Flight Controls.question: To protect seals from damage when installed over a threaded section, the threaded section should be

choices.A: coated with a heavy grease.
choices.B: covered with tape.
choices.C: covered with a suitable sleeve.

Flight Controls.question: What function does the absolute pressure regulator perform in the pneumatic power system?

choices.A: Regulates the compressor outlet air pressure to stabilize the system pressure.
choices.B: Regulates the pneumatic system pressure to protect the moisture separator from internal explosion.
choices.C: Regulates the compressor inlet air to provide a stabilized source of air for the compressor.

Flight Controls.question: If a rigid tube is too short for the flare to reach its seat before tightening, pulling it into place by tightening

choices.A: is acceptable.
choices.B: may distort the flare.
choices.C: may distort the cone.

Flight Controls.question: The internal resistance of a fluid which tends to prevent it from flowing is called

choices.A: volatility.
choices.B: viscosity.
choices.C: stability.

Flight Controls.question: The primary function of the flap overload valve is to

choices.A: prevent the flaps from being lowered at airspeeds which would impose excessive structural loads.
choices.B: cause the flap segments located on opposite sides of the aircraft centerline to extend and retract together so that the aircraft will not become aerodynamically unbalanced to the extent that it becomes uncontrollable.
choices.C: boost normal system pressure to the flaps in order to overcome the air loads acting on the relatively large flap area.

Flight Controls.question: A crossflow valve which is designed to bypass fluid from one side of an actuating cylinder to the other side, under certain conditions, may be found in some aircraft installed in the

choices.C: landing gear system.

Flight Controls.question: Although dents in the heel of a bend are not permissible, they are acceptable in the remainder of a hydraulic tube providing they are less than what percent of the tube diameter?

choices.A: 5 percent.
choices.B: 10 percent.
choices.C: 20 percent.

Flight Controls.question: If the liquid level gauge in a vapor-cycle cooling system indicates a low freon charge, the system should

choices.A: 10 hours.

Environmental Systems.question: Electric circuits are protected from overheating by

Reciprocating Engines.question: What does valve overlap promote?

choices.A: Lower intake manifold pressure and temperatures.

Reciprocating Engines.question: A hissing sound from the exhaust stacks when the propeller is being pulled through manually indicates

Reciprocating Engines.question: If an engine cylinder is to be removed, at what position in the cylinder should the piston be?

choices.A: Bottom dead center.

choices.C: Halfway between top and bottom dead center.

Reciprocating Engines.question: The horsepower developed in the cylinders of a reciprocating engine is known as the

choices.A: shaft horsepower.
choices.B: indicated horsepower.

Reciprocating Engines.question: Engine operating flexibility is the ability of the engine to

choices.C: run smoothly and give the desired performance at all speeds.

Reciprocating Engines.question: Standard aircraft cylinder oversizes usually range from 0.010 inch to 0.030 inch. Oversize on automobile engine cylinders may range up to 0.100 inch. This is because aircraft engine cylinders

choices.C: operate at high temperatures.

Reciprocating Engines.question: By use of a differential pressure compression tester, it is determined that cylinder 3 of a nine-cylinder radial engine will not hold pressure after the crankshaft has been rotated 260 from top dead center compression stroke cylinder 1. How can this indication usually be interpreted?

choices.A: A normal indication.
choices.B: Exhaust valve blow-by.

choices.A: At the end of the exhaust stroke and the beginning of the intake stroke.
choices.B: At the end of the power stroke and the beginning of the exhaust stroke.
choices.C: At the end of the compression stroke and the beginning of the power stroke.

Reciprocating Engines.question: What is an advantage of using metallic-sodium filled exhaust valves in aircraft reciprocating engines?

choices.A: Increased strength and resistance to cracking.
choices.B: Reduced valve operating temperatures.
choices.C: Greater resistance to deterioration at high valve temperatures.

Reciprocating Engines.question: Valve clearance changes on opposed-type engines using hydraulic lifters are accomplished by

choices.A: rocker arm adjustment.

Reciprocating Engines.question: What is likely to occur if a reciprocating engine is operated at high power settings before it is properly warmed up?

Reciprocating Engines.question: Direct mechanical push-pull carburetor heat control linkages should normally be adjusted so that the stop located on the diverter valve will be contacted

Reciprocating Engines.question: Increased water vapor (higher relative humidity) in the incoming air to a reciprocating engine will normally result in which of the following?

Reciprocating Engines.question: Select the Airworthiness Directive applicability statement which applies to an IVO-355 engine, serial number T8164, with 2,100 hours total time and 300 hours since rebuilding.

The purpose of staggered ignition is to compensate for

Reciprocating Engines.question: What carburetor component limits the maximum airflow into the engine at full throttle?

choices.B: remove the seals prior to greasing and reinstall them afterwards.
choices.C: remove one of the two zerk fittings for the blade and grease the blade through the remaining fitting.

Propellers.question: When lubricating a newly overhauled steel hub propeller, the aviation mechanic should lubricate the propeller

choices.A: after 10 hours of operation.
choices.B: at the next 100-hour inspection.
choices.C: after one to two hours of operation.

Propellers.question: The primary purpose of a feathering propeller is to

choices.A: prevent further engine damage when an engine fails in flight.
choices.B: prevent propeller damage when an engine fails in flight.
choices.C: eliminate the drag created by a windmilling propeller when an engine fails in flight.

Propellers.question: What normally prevents a Hartzell Compact propeller from going to feather when the engine is shut down on the ground?

choices.A: Propeller cylinder air pressure.
choices.B: A latch mechanism composed of springs and lock pins.
choices.C: Accumulator provided oil pressure.

Propellers.question: When running-up an engine and testing a newly installed hydromatic propeller, it is necessary to exercise the propeller by moving the governor control through its entire travel several times to

choices.A: seat the blades fully against the low pitch stop.
choices.B: free the dome of any entrapped air.
choices.C: test the maximum RPM setting of the governor.

Propellers.question: Which of the following occurs to cause front cone bottoming during propeller installation?

choices.A: The front cone becomes bottomed in the front propeller hub cone seat before the rear propeller hub cone seat has engaged the rear cone.
choices.B: The front cone enters the front propeller hub cone seat at an angle causing the propeller retaining nut to appear tight when it is only partially tightened.
choices.C: The front cone contacts the ends of the shaft splines, preventing the front and rear cones from being tightened against the cone seats in the propeller hub.

Propellers.question: What is indicated when the front cone bottoms while installing a propeller?

choices.A: Propeller-dome combination is incorrect.
choices.B: Blade angles are incorrect.
choices.C: Rear cone should be moved forward.

Propellers.question: How is the oil pressure delivery on a hydromatic propeller normally stopped after the blades have reached their full-feathered position?

choices.A: Pulling out the feathering push button.
choices.B: Electric cutout pressure switch.
choices.C: Stop lugs in the teeth of the rotating cam.

Propellers.question: The primary purpose of the front and rear cones for propellers that are installed on splined shafts is to

choices.A: position the propeller hub on the splined shaft.
choices.B: prevent metal-to-metal contact between the propeller and the splined shaft.
choices.C: reduce stresses between the splines of the propeller and the splines of the shaft.

Propellers.question: Which of the following statements concerning the installation of a new fixed-pitch wood propeller is true?

choices.A: Inspect the bolts for proper torque after every 50 hours and annual inspection.
choices.B: Install and tighten the bolts to the proper torque during installation; no inspection interval after that.
choices.C: Inspect the bolts for proper torque after the first flight and after the first 25 hours of flying.

Propellers.question: If propeller cones or hub cone seats show evidence of galling and wear, the most likely cause is

choices.A: the pitch change stops were located incorrectly, causing the cone seats to act as the high pitch stop.
choices.B: the propeller retaining nut was not tight enough during previous operation.
choices.C: the front cone was not fully bottomed against the crankshaft splines during installation.

Propellers.question: On aircraft equipped with hydraulically operated constant-speed propellers, all ignition and magneto checking is done with the propeller in which position?

choices.A: HIGH RPM.
choices.B: LOW RPM.
choices.C: High pitch range.

Propellers.question: Oil leakage around the rear cone of a hydromatic propeller usually indicates a defective

choices.A: piston gasket.
choices.B: spider-shaft oil seal.
choices.C: dome-barrel oil seal.

Propellers.question: Maximum taper contact between crankshaft and propeller hub is determined by using

choices.A: bearing blue color transfer.
choices.B: a micrometer.
choices.C: a surface gauge.

Propellers.question: Propeller blade tracking is the process of determining

choices.A: the plane of rotation of the propeller with respect to the aircraft longitudinal axis.
choices.B: that the blade angles are within the specified tolerance of each other.
choices.C: the positions of the tips of the propeller blades relative to each other.

Propellers.question: What is the basic purpose of the three small holes (No. 60 drill) in the tipping of wood propeller blades?

choices.A: To provide a means for inserting balancing shot when necessary.
choices.B: To provide a means for periodically impregnating the blade with preservation materials.
choices.C: To allow the moisture which may collect between the tipping and the wood to escape (vent the tipping).

Propellers.question: A fixed-pitch wooden propeller that has been properly installed and the attachment bolts properly torqued exceeds the out-of-track allowance by 1/16 inch. The excessive out-of-track condition may be corrected by

choices.A: slightly overtightening the attachment bolts adjacent to the most forward blade.
choices.B: discarding the propeller since out-of-track conditions cannot be corrected.
choices.C: placing shims between the inner flange and the propeller.

Propellers.question: Manually feathering a hydromechanical propeller means to

choices.A: block governor oil pressure to the cylinder of the propeller.
choices.B: port governor oil pressure to the cylinder of the propeller.
choices.C: port governor oil pressure from the cylinder of the propeller.

Propellers.question: In what position is the constant-speed propeller control placed to check the magnetos?

choices.A: Full decrease, low propeller blade pitch angle.
choices.B: Full increase, high propeller blade pitch angle.
choices.C: Full increase, low propeller blade pitch angle.

Propellers.question: If a flanged propeller shaft has dowel pins

choices.A: install the propeller so the blades are positioned for hand propping.
choices.B: the propeller can only be installed in a given position.
choices.C: the front cone should be checked for bottoming against the pins.

Propellers.question: Repairs of aluminum alloy adjustable pitch propellers are not permitted to be made on which of the following propeller blade areas?

choices.A: Shank.
choices.B: Face.
choices.C: Back.

Propellers.question: Which of the following methods is used to straighten a bent aluminum propeller blade that is within repairable limits?

choices.A: Careful heating to accomplish straightening, followed by heat treatment to restore original strength.
choices.B: Either hot or cold straightening, depending on the location and severity of damage.
choices.C: Never straighten a damaged propeller.

Propellers.question: It is important that nicks in aluminum alloy propeller blades be repaired as soon as possible in order to

choices.A: maintain equal aerodynamic characteristics between the blades.
choices.B: eliminate stress concentration points.
choices.C: equalize the centrifugal loads between the blades.

Propellers.question: Generally, unless otherwise specified by the manufacturer, repairs of nicks, scratches, gouges, etc., on aluminum propeller blades must be made

choices.A: parallel to the length of the blade.
choices.B: perpendicular to the blade axis.
choices.C: so as to return the damaged area to the original dimensions.

Propellers.question: Minor surface damage located in a repairable area, but not on the leading or trailing edges of aluminum blades, may be repaired by first

choices.A: filing with a riffle file.
choices.B: filing with a half round or flat file.
choices.C: rough sanding and applying a proper filler.

Propellers.question: After proper removal of aluminum blade damage, the affected surface should be polished with

choices.A: fine steel wool or scotch-brite.
choices.B: very fine sandpaper or crocus cloth.
choices.C: soapstone or wire brush.

Propellers.question: When preparing a propeller blade for inspection it should be cleaned with

choices.A: mild soap and water.
choices.B: steel wool.
choices.C: methyl ethyl ketone.

Propellers.question: What method would be used to inspect an aluminum propeller blade when a crack is suspected?

choices.A: Use a bright light.
choices.B: Magnetic particle.
choices.C: Dye-penetrant.

Propellers.question: Removal of propeller blade tips within Type Certificate Data Sheet limits when correcting a defect is

choices.A: a major alteration.
choices.B: a major repair.
choices.C: permitted under the privileges and limitations of a powerplant rating.

Propellers.question: Surface treatment to counter the effects of dye-penetrant inspection on a propeller is accomplished by

choices.A: washing off with solvent.
choices.B: wiping with alcohol.
choices.C: rinse the blade in alodine solution.

Propellers.question: One of the advantages of inspecting an aluminum propeller utilizing dye-penetrant inspection procedure is that

choices.A: defects just below the surface are indicated.
choices.B: it shows whether visible lines and other marks are actually cracks rather than scratches.
choices.C: it indicates overspeed condition.

Propellers.question: The primary reason for careful inspection and prompt repairing of minor surface defects such as scratches, nicks, gouges, etc. on aluminum alloy propellers is to prevent

choices.A: corrosion.
choices.B: unbalanced aerodynamics.
choices.C: fatigue failure.

Propellers.question: Which of the following generally renders an aluminum alloy propeller unrepairable?

choices.A: Blade face surface damage.
choices.B: Leading or trailing edge damage.
choices.C: Transverse cracks of any size.

Propellers.question: Cold straightening a bent aluminum propeller blade may be accomplished by

choices.A: the holder of a mechanic certificate with a powerplant rating.
choices.B: an appropriately rated repair station or the manufacturer.
choices.C: a person working under the supervision of the holder of a mechanic certificate with both airframe and powerplant ratings.

Propellers.question: On twin engine aircraft, the propeller autofeather system is activated when

choices.A: rudder forces increase beyond a set value during an engine-out condition.
choices.B: a propeller overspeed condition is detected.
choices.C: engine power loss results in a propeller thrust drop to a preset value.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a thrust bearing used in most radial engines?

choices.A: Tapered roller.
choices.B: Double-row ball.
choices.C: Deep-groove ball.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the purpose of the safety circlet installed on some valve stems?

choices.A: To hold the valve guide in position.
choices.B: To hold the valve spring retaining washer in position.
choices.C: To prevent valves from falling into the combustion chamber.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following engine servicing operations generally requires engine pre-oiling prior to starting the engine?

choices.A: Engine oil and filter change.
choices.B: Engine installation.
choices.C: Replacement of oil lines.

Turbine Engines.question: At what point in an axial-flow turbojet engine will the highest gas pressures occur?

choices.A: At the turbine entrance.
choices.B: Within the burner section.
choices.C: At the compressor outlet.

Turbine Engines.question: One function of the nozzle diaphragm in a turbine engine is to

choices.A: Decrease the velocity of exhaust gases.
choices.B: Center the fuel spray in the combustion chamber.
choices.C: Direct the flow of gases to strike the turbine blades at the desired angle.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the profile of a turbine engine compressor blade?

choices.A: The leading edge of the blade.
choices.B: A reduced blade tip thickness.
choices.C: The curvature of the blade root.

Turbine Engines.question: The fan rotational speed of a dual axial compressor forward fan engine is the same as the

choices.A: low-pressure compressor.
choices.B: forward turbine wheel.

choices.A: the total inlet pressure.
choices.B: pressure and temperature at station 7.

Turbine Engines.question: The blending of blades and vanes in a turbine engine

choices.A: is usually accomplished only at engine overhaul.
choices.B: should be performed parallel to the length of the blade using smooth contours to minimize stress points.

choices.A: Combustion section.
choices.B: Compressor section.
choices.C: Diffuser section.

Turbine Engines.question: In a gas turbine engine, combustion occurs at a constant

choices.A: volume.
choices.B: pressure.
choices.C: density.

Turbine Engines.question: Which statement is true regarding jet engines?

choices.A: At the lower engine speeds, thrust increases rapidly with small increases in RPM.
choices.B: At the higher engine speeds, thrust increases rapidly with small increases in RPM.

choices.A: low-pressure rotor will increase in speed as the compressor load decreases in the lower density air.
choices.B: throttle must be retarded to prevent overspeeding of the high-pressure rotor due to the lower density air.
choices.C: low-pressure rotor will decrease in speed as the compressor load decreases in the lower density air.

choices.A: decrease the velocity of the heated gases flowing past this point.
choices.B: direct the flow of gases parallel to the vertical line of the turbine blades.
choices.C: increase the velocity of the heated gases flowing past this point.

choices.A: At the outlet of the tailpipe section.
choices.B: At the entrance of the turbine section.
choices.C: In the entrance of the burner section.

choices.A: increase and the velocity to decrease.

choices.A: To straighten airflow to eliminate turbulence.

choices.B: Layout dye.

Turbine Engines.question: What are the two basic elements of the turbine section in a turbine engine?

choices.A: Impeller and diffuser.
choices.B: Hot and cold.

Turbine Engines.question: Some engine manufacturers of twin spool gas turbine engines identify turbine discharge pressure in their maintenance manuals as

choices.A: Pt7.
choices.B: Pt2.
choices.C: Tt7.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following statements is true regarding the basic gas turbine engine?

choices.A: The engine is divided into three sections: cold, neutral, and hot sections.

choices.A: layout dye, commercial felt tip marker, or chalk.

choices.A: The FAA.
choices.B: The component manufacturer.

choices.B: Teflon and synthetic rubber.

Turbine Engines.question: How does a dual axial-flow compressor improve the efficiency of a turbojet engine?

choices.A: More turbine wheels can be used.

Turbine Engines.question: Three types of turbine blades are

choices.A: impulse, converging, and impulse-converging.
choices.B: impulse, reaction, and impulse-reaction.

Turbine Engines.question: Which statements are true regarding aircraft engine propulsion?

choices.A: An engine driven propeller imparts a relatively small amount of acceleration to a large mass of air.
choices.B: An engine driven propeller imparts a relatively large amount of acceleration to a smaller mass of air.

Turbine Engines.question: An advantage of the axial-flow compressor is its

choices.A: low starting power requirements.
choices.B: low weight.
choices.C: high peak efficiency.

choices.A: Stabilize the pressure of the airflow.
choices.B: Control the direction of the airflow.
choices.C: Increase the velocity of the airflow.

choices.A: To increase pressure and reduce velocity.

choices.A: Can.
choices.B: Can-annular.
choices.C: Annular.

choices.A: the burner section and the turbine section.

choices.A: the high probability of carbon deposits.

choices.A: Compressor inlet air temperature.

Turbine Engines.question: Jet engine turbine blades removed for detailed inspection must be reinstalled in

choices.A: a specified slot 180 away.

choices.A: pressure rise per stage.
choices.B: ram efficiency.
choices.C: peak efficiency.

Turbine Engines.question: The highest heat-to-metal contact in a jet engine is the

choices.B: turbine inlet guide vanes.
choices.C: turbine blades.

Turbine Engines.question: Which two elements make up the axial-flow compressor assembly?

choices.A: Rotor and stator.
choices.B: Compressor and manifold.
choices.C: Stator and diffuser.

Turbine Engines.question: The two types of centrifugal compressor impellers are

choices.A: single entry and double entry.
choices.B: rotor and stator.
choices.C: impeller and diffuser.

choices.A: buckets.

choices.A: prevent engine oil contamination and subsequent engine bearing wear or damage.

Turbine Engines.question: A purpose of the shrouds on the turbine blades of an axial-flow engine is to

Turbine Engines.question: In a dual axial-flow compressor, the first stage turbine drives

choices.B: N(1) compressor.

choices.A: Ignition, starter, fuel.

Turbine Engines.question: The stator vanes in an axial-flow compressor

choices.A: convert velocity energy into pressure energy.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the primary advantage of an axial-flow compressor over a centrifugal compressor?

choices.A: High frontal area.

Turbine Engines.question: The purpose of a bleed valve, located in the beginning stages of the compressor, in an aircraft gas turbine engine is to

Turbine Engines.question: What is meant by a double-entry centrifugal compressor?

Turbine Engines.question: What is the major function of the turbine assembly in a turbojet engine?

choices.A: Directs the gases in the proper direction to the tailpipe.

Turbine Engines.question: Stator blades in the compressor section of an axial-flow turbine engine

choices.A: increase the air velocity and prevent swirling.
choices.B: straighten the airflow and accelerate it.

Turbine Engines.question: A gas turbine engine comprises which three main sections?

choices.A: Compressor, diffuser, and stator.
choices.B: Turbine, combustion, and stator.

Turbine Engines.question: What type of turbine blade is most commonly used in aircraft jet engines?

choices.A: Reaction.
choices.B: Impulse.
choices.C: Impulse-reaction.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the primary factor which controls the pressure ratio of an axial-flow compressor?

choices.B: Compressor inlet pressure.

Turbine Engines.question: The non-rotating axial-flow compressor airfoils in an aircraft gas turbine engine, are called

choices.B: stator vanes.
choices.C: disc rims.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following statements is true regarding a turbine engine axial-flow compressor?

Turbine Engines.question: The air passing through the combustion chamber of a turbine engine is

choices.A: used to support combustion and to cool the engine.
choices.B: entirely combined with fuel and burned.
choices.C: speeded up and heated by the action of the turbines.

Turbine Engines.question: The stators in the turbine section of a gas turbine engine

choices.A: increase the velocity of the gas flow.
choices.B: decrease the velocity of the gas flow.
choices.C: increase the pressure of the gas flow.

Turbine Engines.question: The compressor stators in a gas turbine engine act as diffusers to

choices.A: decrease the velocity of the gas flow.
choices.B: increase the velocity of the gas flow.
choices.C: increase the velocity and decrease the pressure of the gas.

Turbine Engines.question: The procedure for removing the accumulation of dirt deposits on compressor blades is called

choices.A: the soak method.
choices.B: field cleaning.
choices.C: the purging process.

Turbine Engines.question: A turbine engine hot section is particularly susceptible to which kind of damage?

choices.A: Erosion.
choices.B: Cracking.
choices.C: Elongation.

Turbine Engines.question: Dirt particles in the air being introduced into the compressor of a turbine engine will form a coating on all but which of the following?

choices.A: Turbine blades.
choices.B: Casings.
choices.C: Inlet guide vanes.

Turbine Engines.question: Severe rubbing of turbine engine compressor blades will usually cause

choices.A: bowing.
choices.B: cracking.
choices.C: galling.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following influences the operation of an automatic fuel control unit on a turbojet engine?

choices.A: Burner pressure.
choices.B: Mixture control position.
choices.C: Exhaust gas temperature.

Turbine Engines.question: If a turbine engine is unable to reach takeoff EPR before its EGT limit is reached, this is an indication that the

choices.A: fuel control must be replaced.
choices.B: EGT controller is out of adjustment.
choices.C: compressor may be contaminated or damaged.

Turbine Engines.question: If the RPM of an axial-flow compressor remains constant, the angle of attack of the rotor blades can be changed by

choices.A: changing the velocity of the airflow.
choices.B: changing the compressor diameter.
choices.C: increasing the pressure ratio.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following variables affect the inlet air density of a turbine engine?

choices.A: Speed of the aircraft, compression ratio, and ambient temperature.
choices.B: Speed of the aircraft, altitude of the aircraft, and ambient temperature.
choices.C: Compression ratio, turbine inlet temperature, and turbine and compressor efficiency.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following factors affect the thermal efficiency of a turbine engine?

choices.A: Ambient temperature, speed of the aircraft, and altitude of the aircraft.
choices.B: Turbine inlet temperature, compression ratio, and turbine and compressor efficiency.
choices.C: Turbine inlet temperature and ambient temperature.

Turbine Engines.question: Why do some turbine engines have more than one turbine wheel attached to a single shaft?

choices.A: To facilitate balancing of the turbine assembly.
choices.B: To help stabilize the pressure between the compressor and the turbine.
choices.C: To extract more power from the exhaust gases than a single wheel can absorb.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following types of combustion sections are used in aircraft turbine engines?

choices.A: Annular, variable, and cascade vane.
choices.B: Can, multiple-can, and variable.
choices.C: Multiple-can, annular, and can-annular.

Turbine Engines.question: A cool-off period prior to shutdown of a turbine engine is accomplished in order to

choices.A: allow the turbine wheel to cool before the case contracts around it.
choices.B: prevent vapor lock in the fuel control and/or fuel lines.
choices.C: prevent seizure of the engine bearings.

Turbine Engines.question: What type igniter plug is used in the low tension ignition system of an aircraft turbofan engine?

choices.A: Low voltage, high amperage glow plug.
choices.B: Self-ionizing or shunted-gap type plug.
choices.C: Recessed surface gap plug.

Turbine Engines.question: What is meant by a shrouded turbine?

choices.A: The turbine blades are shaped so that their ends form a band or shroud.
choices.B: The turbine wheel is enclosed by a protective shroud to contain the blades in case of failure.
choices.C: The turbine wheel has a shroud or duct which provides cooling air to the turbine blades.

Turbine Engines.question: What term is used to describe a permanent and cumulative deformation of the turbine blades of a turbojet engine?

choices.A: Stretch.
choices.B: Distortion.
choices.C: Creep.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the purpose of the dump valve used on aircraft gas turbine engines?

choices.A: The fuel is quickly cut off to the nozzles and the manifolds are drained preventing fuel boiling off as a result of residual engine heat.
choices.B: The valve controls compressor stall by dumping compressor bleed air from the compressor discharge port under certain conditions.
choices.C: Maintains minimum fuel pressure to the engine fuel control unit inlet and dumps excessive fuel back to the inlet of the engine-driven fuel pump.

Turbine Engines.question: At what stage in a turbine engine are gas pressures the greatest?

choices.A: Compressor inlet.
choices.B: Turbine outlet.
choices.C: Compressor outlet.

Turbine Engines.question: In what section of a turbojet engine is the jet nozzle located?

choices.A: Combustion.
choices.B: Turbine.
choices.C: Exhaust.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following statements is true regarding contaminants in the compressor?

choices.A: Accumulation of contaminants in the compressor of a turbojet engine increases aerodynamic efficiency of the blades.
choices.B: Two common methods for removing dirt deposits from turbojet engine compressor blades are a fluid wash and an abrasive grit blast.
choices.C: The only way to remove dirt deposits is an abrasive grit blast.

Turbine Engines.question: Hot spots in the combustion section of a turbojet engine are possible indicators of

choices.A: faulty igniter plugs.
choices.B: dirty compressor blades.
choices.C: malfunctioning fuel nozzles.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following can cause fan blade shingling in a turbofan engine?

choices.A: Engine overtemperature.
choices.B: Large, rapid throttle movements.
choices.C: Engine overspeed and/or FOD.

Turbine Engines.question: Compressor stall is caused by

choices.A: a low angle of attack airflow through the first stages of compression.
choices.B: a high angle of attack airflow through the first stages of compression.
choices.C: rapid engine deceleration.

Turbine Engines.question: A condition known as 'hot streaking' in turbine engines is caused by

choices.A: a partially clogged fuel nozzle.
choices.B: a misaligned combustion liner.
choices.C: excessive fuel flow.

Turbine Engines.question: Determine which portion of the AD is applicable for Model O-690 series engine, serial No. 5863-40 with 283 hours' time in service. This is the compliance portion of an FAA Airworthiness Directive. Compliance required as indicated: (A) For model O-690 series engines, serial Nos. 101-40 through 5264-40 and IO-690 series engines, serial Nos. 101-48 through 423-48, compliance with (C) required within 25 hours' time in service after the effective date of this AD and every 100 hours' time in service thereafter. (B) For Model O-690 series engines, serial Nos. 5265-40 through 6129-40 and IO-690 series engines, serial Nos. 424-48 through 551-48, compliance with (C) required as follows: (1) Within 25 hours' time in service after the effective date of this AD and every 100 hours' time in service thereafter for engines with more than 275 hours' time in service on the effective date of this AD. (2) Prior to the accumulation of 300 hours total time in service and every 100 hours' time in service thereafter for engines with 275 hours or less time in service on the effective date of this AD. (C) Inspect the oil pump drive shaft (P/N 67512) on applicable engines in accordance with instructions contained in Connin Service Bulletin No. 295. Any shafts which are found to be damaged shall be replaced before further flight. These inspections shall be continued until Connin P/N 67512 (redesigned) or P/N 74641 oil pump drive shaft is installed at which time the inspections may be discontinued.
Where would one find type design information for an R1830-92 engine certificated under the Civil Air Regulations (CAR) and installed on a DC-3?

choices.A: The Aircraft Specifications and Type Certificate Data Sheet.
choices.B: The Aircraft Engine Specifications.
choices.C: The Aircraft Engine Type Certificate Handbook.

Turbine Engines.question: Each powerplant installed on an airplane with a Standard Airworthiness Certificate must have been

choices.A: type certificated.
choices.B: manufactured under the TSO system.
choices.C: originally certificated for that aircraft.

Turbine Engines.question: What publication contains the mandatory replacement time for parts of a turbine engine?

choices.A: Engine manufacturer's service instructions.
choices.B: 14 CFR Part 43
choices.C: Engine manufacturer's maintenance manual.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following conditions is usually not acceptable to any extent in turbine blades?

choices.A: Cracks.
choices.B: Pits.
choices.C: Dents.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following statements is true?

choices.A: Serviceability limits for turbine blades are much more stringent than those for turbine nozzle vanes.
choices.B: A limited number of small nicks and dents can usually be permitted in any area of a turbine blade.
choices.C: Serviceability limits for turbine nozzle vanes are much more stringent than those for turbine blades.

Turbine Engines.question: Powerplant instrument range markings

choices.A: show whether the current state of powerplant operation is abnormal, unacceptable for a limited time, or authorized.
choices.B: are based on installed engine operating limits, which may exceed those limits shown on the engine Type Certificate Data Sheet.
choices.C: show whether the current state of powerplant operation is normal, acceptable for a limited time, or unauthorized.

Turbine Engines.question: Thermocouple leads

choices.A: may be installed with either lead to either post of the indicator.
choices.B: are designed for a specific installation and may not be altered.
choices.C: may be repaired using solderless connectors.

Turbine Engines.question: The engine pressure ratio (EPR) is a ratio of the

choices.A: exhaust gas pressure to the engine inlet air pressure and indicates the thrust produced.
choices.B: exhaust gas pressure to the engine inlet air pressure and indicates volumetric efficiency.
choices.C: exhaust gas pressure to the engine exhaust pressure and indicates the thrust produced.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following statements is true?

choices.A: When a turbine engine indicates low EGT for a particular EPR, it means that the engine is out of trim.
choices.B: Some turbine-powered aircraft use EGT as the primary indicator of thrust produced.
choices.C: When a turbine engine indicates high EGT for a particular EPR, it means that the engine is out of trim.

Turbine Engines.question: Turbine engine EGT thermocouples are constructed of

choices.A: iron and constantan.
choices.B: copper and constantan.
choices.C: chromel and alumel.

Turbine Engines.question: What instrument on a gas turbine engine should be monitored to minimize the possibility of a 'hot' start?

choices.A: RPM indicator.
choices.B: Turbine inlet temperature.
choices.C: Torquemeter.

Turbine Engines.question: In regard to using a turbine engine oil analysis program, which of the following is NOT true?

choices.A: Generally, an accurate trend forecast may be made after an engine's first oil sample analysis.
choices.B: It is best to start an oil analysis program on an engine when it is new.
choices.C: A successful oil analysis program should be run over an engine's total operating life so that normal trends can be established.

Turbine Engines.question: On an aircraft turbine engine, operating at a constant power, the application of engine anti-icing will result in

choices.A: noticeable shift in EPR.
choices.B: a false EPR reading.
choices.C: an increase in EPR.

Turbine Engines.question: Engine pressure ratio is the total pressure ratio between the

choices.A: aft end of the compressor and the aft end of the turbine.
choices.B: front of the compressor and the rear of the turbine.
choices.C: front of the engine inlet and the aft end of the compressor.

Turbine Engines.question: What would be the possible cause if a gas turbine engine has high exhaust gas temperature, high fuel flow, and low RPM at all engine power settings?

choices.A: Fuel control out of adjustment.
choices.B: Loose or corroded thermocouple probes for the EGT indicator.
choices.C: Turbine damage or loss of turbine efficiency.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the primary purpose of the tachometer on an axial-compressor turbine engine?

choices.A: Monitor engine RPM during cruise conditions.
choices.B: It is the most accurate instrument for establishing thrust settings under all conditions.
choices.C: Monitor engine RPM during starting and to indicate overspeed conditions.

Turbine Engines.question: The engine pressure ratio (EPR) indicator is a direct indication of

choices.A: engine thrust being produced.
choices.B: pressure ratio between the front and aft end of the compressor.
choices.C: ratio of engine RPM to compressor pressure.

Turbine Engines.question: The exhaust gas temperature (EGT) indicator on a gas turbine engine provides a relative indication of the

choices.A: exhaust temperature.
choices.B: temperature of the exhaust gases as they pass the exhaust cone.
choices.C: turbine inlet temperature.

Turbine Engines.question: What instrument indicates the thrust of a gas turbine engine?

choices.A: Exhaust gas temperature indicator.
choices.B: Turbine inlet temperature indicator.
choices.C: Engine pressure ratio indicator.

Turbine Engines.question: In a turbine engine, where is the turbine discharge pressure indicator sensor located?

choices.A: At the aft end of the compressor section.
choices.B: At a location in the exhaust cone that is determined to be subjected to the highest pressures.
choices.C: Immediately aft of the last turbine stage.

Turbine Engines.question: In what units are turbine engine tachometers calibrated?

choices.A: Percent of engine RPM.
choices.B: Actual engine RPM.
choices.C: Percent of engine pressure ratio.

Turbine Engines.question: Why does one type of Fenwal fire detection system use spot detectors wired in parallel between two separate circuits?

choices.A: To provide an installation that is equal to two separate systems: a primary system and a secondary, or back-up system.
choices.B: So that a double fault may exist in the system without sounding a false alarm.
choices.C: So that a single fault may exist in the system without sounding a false alarm.

Turbine Engines.question: A high surge of current is required when a DC electric motor is first started. As the speed of the motor increases,

choices.A: the counter EMF decreases proportionally.
choices.B: the applied EMF increases proportionally.
choices.C: the counter EMF builds up and opposes the applied EMF, thus reducing the current flow through the armature.

Turbine Engines.question: Why is a constant-speed drive used to control the speed of some aircraft engine-driven generators?

choices.A: So that the voltage output of the generator will remain within limits.
choices.B: To eliminate uncontrolled surges of current to the electrical system.
choices.C: So that the frequency of the alternating current output will remain constant.

Turbine Engines.question: Why is it unnecessary to flash the field of the exciter on a brushless alternator?

choices.A: The exciter is constantly charged by battery voltage.
choices.B: Brushless alternators do not have exciters.
choices.C: Permanent magnets are installed in the main field poles.

Turbine Engines.question: One way that the automatic ignition relight systems are activated on gas turbine engines is by a

choices.A: drop in compressor discharge pressure.
choices.B: sensing switch located in the tailpipe.
choices.C: drop in fuel flow.

Turbine Engines.question: When installing an electrical switch, under which of the following conditions should the switch be derated from its nominal current rating?

choices.A: Conductive circuits.
choices.B: Capacitive circuits.
choices.C: Direct current motor circuits.

Turbine Engines.question: In order to reduce the possibility of ground shorting the circuits when the connectors are separated for maintenance, the AN and MS electrical connectors should be installed with the

choices.A: socket section on the ground side of the electrical circuit.
choices.B: pin section on the ground side of the electrical circuit.
choices.C: pin section on the positive side of the electrical circuit.

Turbine Engines.question: When a 28-volt, 75-ampere generator is installed on an aircraft, an electrical load analysis ground check is performed and it is determined that the battery is furnishing 57 amperes to the system, with all electrical equipment operating. This indicates

choices.A: the load exceeds the maximum system percentage capacity.
choices.B: that the generator load will exceed the generator limit.
choices.C: the load will be within the generator load limit.

Turbine Engines.question: Bonding jumpers should be designed and installed in such a manner that they

choices.A: are not subjected to flexing by relative motion of airframe or engine components.
choices.B: provide a low electrical resistance in the ground circuit.
choices.C: prevent buildup of a static electrical charge between the airframe and the surrounding atmosphere.

Turbine Engines.question: When the starter switch to the aircraft gas turbine engine starter-generator is energized and the engine fails to rotate, one of the probable causes would be the

choices.A: power lever switch is defective.
choices.B: undercurrent solenoid contacts are defective.
choices.C: starter solenoid is defective.

Turbine Engines.question: ON-OFF two position engine electrical switches should be installed

choices.A: so that the toggle will move in the same direction as the desired motion of the unit controlled.
choices.B: under a guard.

Turbine Engines.question: When selecting an electrical switch for installation in an aircraft circuit utilizing a direct current motor,

choices.A: a switch designed for DC should be chosen.
choices.B: a derating factor should be applied.
choices.C: only switches with screw-type terminal connections should be used.

Turbine Engines.question: When installing electrical wiring parallel to a fuel line, the wiring should be

choices.A: below the fuel line.
choices.B: beside the fuel line.
choices.C: above the fuel line.

Turbine Engines.question: How many basic types of circuit breakers are used in powerplant installation electrical systems?

choices.A: Two.
choices.B: Three.
choices.C: Four.

Turbine Engines.question: Electrical circuit protection devices are installed primarily to protect the

choices.A: switches.
choices.B: units.
choices.C: wiring.

Turbine Engines.question: A term commonly used when two or more electrical terminals are installed on a single lug of a terminal strip is

choices.A: strapping.
choices.B: piggy backing.
choices.C: stacking.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following statements is true?

choices.A: Gas turbine and reciprocating engine oils can be mixed or used interchangeably.
choices.B: Most gas turbine engine oils are synthetic.
choices.C: Most gas turbine engine oils are straight mineral or ashless dispersant.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of these characteristics is desirable in turbine engine oil?

choices.A: Low flash point.
choices.B: High flash point.
choices.C: High volatility.

Turbine Engines.question: Manufacturers normally require turbine engine oil servicing within a short time after engine shutdown primarily to

choices.A: prevent overservicing.
choices.B: help dilute and neutralize any contaminants that may already be present in the engine's oil system.
choices.C: provide a better indication of any oil leaks in the system.

Turbine Engines.question: How is oil cooled in gas turbine engines?

choices.A: Fuel may not be used to cool oil in gas turbine engines.
choices.B: Ram air may not be used to cool oil in gas turbine engines.
choices.C: Ram air may be used to cool oil in gas turbine engines.

Turbine Engines.question: The oil dampened main bearing utilized in some turbine engines is used to

choices.A: provide lubrication of bearings from the beginning of starting rotation until normal oil pressure is established.
choices.B: provide an oil film between the outer race and the bearing housing in order to reduce vibration tendencies in the rotor system, and to allow for slight misalignment.
choices.C: dampen surges in oil pressure to the bearings.

Turbine Engines.question: In an axial-flow turbine engine, compressor bleed air is sometimes used to aid in cooling the

choices.A: fuel.
choices.B: inlet guide vanes.
choices.C: turbine, vanes, blades, and bearings.

Turbine Engines.question: Oil picks up the most heat from which of the following turbine engine components?

choices.A: Rotor coupling.
choices.B: Compressor bearing.
choices.C: Turbine bearing.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following is a function of the fuel/oil heat exchanger on a turbojet engine?

choices.A: Aerates the fuel.
choices.B: Emulsifies the oil.
choices.C: Increases fuel temperature.

Turbine Engines.question: According to the Federal Aviation Regulations, oil tank fillers on turbine engines must be marked with the word

choices.A: 'oil' and the type and grade of oil specified by the manufacturer.
choices.B: 'oil' and tank capacity.
choices.C: 'oil.'

Turbine Engines.question: After making a welded repair to a pressurized-type turbine engine oil tank, the tank should be pressure checked to

choices.A: not less than 5 psi plus the maximum operating pressure of the tank.
choices.B: not less than 5 psi plus the average operating pressure of the tank.
choices.C: 5 psi.

Turbine Engines.question: Why are fixed orifice nozzles used in the lubrication system of gas turbine engines?

choices.A: To provide a relatively constant oil flow to the main bearings at all engine speeds.
choices.B: To keep back pressure on the oil pump, thus preventing an air lock.
choices.C: To protect the oil seals by preventing excessive pressure from entering the bearing cavities.

Turbine Engines.question: Possible failure related ferrous-metal particles in turbine engine oil cause an (electrical) indicating-type magnetic chip detector to indicate their presence by

choices.A: disturbing the magnetic lines of flux around the detector tip.
choices.B: bridging the gap between the detector center (positive) electrode and the ground electrode.
choices.C: generating a small electric current that is caused by the particles being in contact with the dissimilar metal of the detector tip.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the primary purpose of the oil breather pressurization system that is used on turbine engines?

choices.A: Prevents foaming of the oil.
choices.B: Allows aeration of the oil for better lubrication because of the air/oil mist.
choices.C: Provides a proper oil spray pattern from the main bearing oil jets.

Turbine Engines.question: The purpose of directing bleed air to the outer turbine case on some engines is to

choices.A: provide optimum turbine blade tip clearance by controlling thermal expansion.
choices.B: provide up to 100 percent kinetic energy extraction from the flowing gases.
choices.C: allow operation in a thermal environment 600 to 800F above the temperature limits of turbine blade and vane alloys.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the primary purpose of the hopper located in the oil supply tank of some dry sump engine installations?

choices.A: To reduce the time required to warm the oil to operating temperatures.
choices.B: To reduce surface aeration of the hot oil and thus reduce oxidation and the formation of sludge and varnish.
choices.C: To impart a centrifugal motion to the oil entering the tank so that the foreign particles in the oil will separate more readily.

Turbine Engines.question: The vent line connecting the oil supply tank and the engine in some dry sump engine installations permits

choices.A: pressurization of the oil supply to prevent cavitation of the oil supply pump.
choices.B: oil vapors from the engine to be condensed and drained into the oil supply tank.
choices.C: the oil tank to be vented through the normal engine vent.

Turbine Engines.question: The purpose of a relief valve installed in the tank venting system of a turbine engine oil tank is to

choices.A: prevent oil pump cavitation by maintaining a constant pressure on the oil pump inlet.
choices.B: maintain internal tank air pressure at the ambient atmospheric level regardless of altitude or rate of change in altitude.
choices.C: maintain a positive internal pressure in the oil tank after shutdown to prevent oil pump cavitation on engine start.

Turbine Engines.question: As a general rule, a small amount of small fuzzy particles or gray metallic paste on a turbine engine magnetic chip detector

choices.A: is considered to be the result of normal wear.
choices.B: indicates an imminent component failure.
choices.C: indicates accelerated generalized wear.

Turbine Engines.question: The capacitor-type ignition system is used almost universally on turbine engines primarily because of its high voltage and

choices.A: low amperage.
choices.B: long life.
choices.C: high-heat intensity.

Turbine Engines.question: How does the ignition system of a gas turbine engine differ from that of a reciprocating engine?

choices.A: One igniter plug is used in each combustion chamber.
choices.B: Magneto-to-engine timing is not critical.
choices.C: A high-energy spark is required for ignition.

Turbine Engines.question: In a turbine engine DC capacitor discharge ignition system, where are the high-voltage pulses formed?

choices.A: At the breaker.
choices.B: At the triggering transformer.
choices.C: At the rectifier.

Turbine Engines.question: If it is found that a shielded ignition system does not adequately reduce ignition noise, it may be necessary to install

choices.A: a second layer of shielding.
choices.B: a filter between the magneto and magneto switch.
choices.C: bonding wires from the shielding to ground.

Turbine Engines.question: The constrained-gap igniter plug used in some gas turbine engines operates at a cooler temperature because

choices.A: it projects into the combustion chamber.
choices.B: the applied voltage is less.
choices.C: the construction is such that the spark occurs beyond the face of the combustion chamber liner.

Turbine Engines.question: Generally, when removing a turbine engine igniter plug, in order to eliminate the possibility of the technician receiving a lethal shock, the ignition switch is turned off and

choices.A: disconnected from the power supply circuit.
choices.B: the igniter lead is disconnected from the plug and the center electrode grounded to the engine after disconnecting the transformer-exciter input lead and waiting the prescribed time.
choices.C: the transformer-exciter input lead is disconnected and the center electrode grounded to the engine after disconnecting the igniter lead from the plug and waiting the prescribed time.

Turbine Engines.question: Great caution should be exercised in handling damaged hermetically sealed turbine engine igniter transformer units because

choices.A: compounds in the unit may become a fire or explosion hazard when exposed to the air.
choices.B: some contain radioactive material.
choices.C: some contain toxic chemicals.

Turbine Engines.question: Igniter plugs used in turbine engines have a long service life because they

choices.A: have a high intensity spark.
choices.B: operate continuously with a lower spark.
choices.C: do not require continuous operation.

Turbine Engines.question: Which statement is correct regarding the ignition system of a turbine engine?

choices.A: The system is normally de-energized as soon as the engine starts.
choices.B: It is energized during the starting and warmup periods only.
choices.C: The system generally includes a polar inductor-type magneto.

Turbine Engines.question: Why do turbine engine ignition systems require high energy?

choices.A: To ignite the fuel under conditions of high altitude and high temperatures.
choices.B: Because the applied voltage is much greater.
choices.C: To ignite the fuel under conditions of high altitude and low temperatures.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following are included in a typical turbine engine ignition system?

choices.A: Two igniter plugs, two transformers, and two intermediate ignition leads.
choices.B: Two igniter plugs, one exciter unit, and two low-tension igniter leads.
choices.C: Two igniter plugs, one exciter unit, and two high-tension igniter leads.

Turbine Engines.question: The primary advantage of pneumatic (air turbine) starters over comparable electric starters for turbine engines is

choices.A: a significant lack of wear to the commutator.
choices.B: that it does not disengage until the engine reaches idle.
choices.C: its high power-to-weight ratio.

Turbine Engines.question: Pneumatic starters are usually designed with what types of airflow impingement systems?

choices.A: Radial inward flow turbine and axial-flow turbine.
choices.B: Centrifugal compressor and axial-flow compressor.
choices.C: Double entry centrifugal outward flow and axial-flow turbines.

Turbine Engines.question: What factor is not used in the operation of an aircraft gas turbine engine fuel control unit?

choices.A: Compressor inlet air temperature.
choices.B: Mixture control position.
choices.C: Power lever position.

Turbine Engines.question: In order to stabilize cams, springs, and linkages within the fuel control, manufacturers generally recommend that all final turbine engine trim adjustments be made in the

choices.A: increase direction.
choices.B: decrease direction.
choices.C: decrease direction after over-adjustment.

Turbine Engines.question: When trimming a turbine engine, the fuel control is adjusted to

choices.A: produce as much power as the engine is capable of producing.
choices.B: set idle RPM and maximum speed or EPR.
choices.C: allow the engine to produce maximum RPM without regard to power output.

Turbine Engines.question: The active clearance control (ACC) portion of an EEC system aids turbine engine efficiency by

choices.A: adjusting stator vane position according to operating conditions and power requirements.
choices.B: ensuring turbine blade to engine case clearances are kept to a minimum by controlling case temperatures.
choices.C: automatically adjusting engine speed to maintain a desired EPR.

Turbine Engines.question: What should be checked/changed to ensure the validity of a turbine engine performance check if an alternate fuel is to be used?

choices.A: Fuel specific gravity setting.
choices.B: Maximum RPM adjustment.
choices.C: EPR gauge calibration.

Turbine Engines.question: For what primary purpose is a turbine engine fuel control unit trimmed?

choices.B: To properly position the power levers.
choices.C: To adjust the idle RPM.

Turbine Engines.question: Which type of fuel control is used on most of today's turbine engines?

choices.A: Electromechanical.
choices.B: Mechanical.
choices.C: Hydromechanical or electronic.

Turbine Engines.question: Under which of the following conditions will the trimming of a turbine engine be most accurate?

choices.A: High wind and high moisture.
choices.B: High moisture and low wind.
choices.C: No wind and low moisture.

Turbine Engines.question: What effect does high atmospheric humidity have on the operation of a jet engine?

choices.A: Decreases engine pressure ratio.
choices.B: Decreases compressor and turbine RPM.

Turbine Engines.question: Which of the following is NOT an input parameter for a turbine engine fuel control unit?

choices.B: Compressor inlet temperature.

choices.C: kerosene does not contain any water.

Turbine Engines.question: What are the principal advantages of the duplex fuel nozzle used in many turbine engines?

choices.A: Restricts the amount of fuel flow to a level where more efficient and complete burning of the fuel is achieved.
choices.B: Provides better atomization and uniform flow pattern.
choices.C: Allows a wider range of fuels and filters to be used.

Turbine Engines.question: It is necessary to control acceleration and deceleration rates in turbine engines in order to

choices.A: prevent blowout or die-out.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the purpose of the flow divider in a turbine engine duplex fuel nozzle?

choices.A: Allows an alternate flow of fuel if the primary flow clogs or is restricted.

choices.A: Exhaust bypass assembly, compressor assembly, and density controller.

Turbine Engines.question: The purpose of a bellmouth compressor inlet is to

Turbine Engines.question: What method(s) is/are used to provide clean air to the engines of helicopters and turboprop airplanes that have particle (sand and ice) separators installed?

Turbine Engines.question: The vortex dissipators installed on some turbine-powered aircraft to prevent engine FOD utilize

Turbine Engines.question: The component(s) in a turbine engine that operate(s) at the highest temperatures is/are the

choices.A: first stage turbine nozzle guide vanes.

Turbine Engines.question: Which statement is true regarding the air passing through the combustion section of a jet engine?

Turbine Engines.question: The hot section of a turbine engine is particularly susceptible to which of the following kind of damage?

choices.A: Galling.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the purpose of a slip joint in an exhaust collector ring?

choices.A: It aids in alignment and absorbs expansion.

Turbine Engines.question: On turbojet powered airplanes, thrust reversers are capable of producing between

choices.A: hot gas re-ingestion, compressor stalls, and sand or other foreign object ingestion.

Turbine Engines.question: What is the proper operating sequence when using thrust reversers to slow an aircraft after landing?

choices.B: Retard thrust levers to ground idle, raise thrust reverser levers as required, and retard thrust reverser levers to ground idle.

choices.B: driven by a single shaft.

Turbine Engines.question: What type of combustion chamber is used in a turbine engine when engine length is critical?

choices.A: Can-annular.
choices.B: Annular.
choices.C: Reverse-flow.

choices.B: aircraft maintenance manual (MM).

Ignition and Starting Systems.question: Which of the following breaker point characteristics is associated with a faulty capacitor?

Ignition and Starting Systems.question: How are most radial engine spark plug wires connected to the distributor block?

Ignition and Starting Systems.question: What should be used to clean grease or carbon tracks from capacitors or coils that are used in magnetos?

Ignition and Starting Systems.question: The electrical circuit from the spark plug back to the magneto is completed by grounding through the

choices.B: P-lead.

Ignition and Starting Systems.question: Spark plugs are considered worn out when the

choices.A: Contact with the air converts the liquid into snow and gas which smothers the flame.
choices.B: By lowering the temperature to a point where combustion will not take place.
choices.C: The high pressure spray lowers the temperature and blows out the fire.

Engine Fire Protection Systems.question: The pulling out (or down) of an illuminated fire handle in a typical large jet aircraft fire protection system commonly accomplishes what events?

choices.A: Closes all firewall shutoff valves, disconnects the generator, and discharges a fire bottle.
